meta.title: How an Individual Is Guided - assignment census v2.0
meta.version_note: v1.2 (2026-06-14): three-tier axis. Added a per-instance `tier` (sutta / abhidhamma / para-canon / commentary) and a per-cell `warrant_tier` for the 15 decidable assignment cells; recomputed aggregates (by_tier, criterion_x_tier, mode_x_tier). The four understanding-types (cst-abh03m2.mul-014) are Puggalapaññatti = abhidhamma, not sutta. The H0/H1 count is unchanged (8/7); the four former 'mūla' warrant cells re-split to sutta (4) + abhidhamma (0), the four para-canonical cells unchanged. Earlier v1.1 note retained below.

h0_h1.reading: Descriptive coding output over the 15 decidable assignment cells (F1+F3): 8 of the 15 cells trace to a canonical warrant (H0) and 7 do not (H1). The split is a restatement of warrant-present versus warrant-absent under the codebook, not a test the data could have failed: there are 0 identity violations and the figures are pinned by the build. Read as a coding result, not as an independent confirmation of any prior expectation. Of the 8 warranted cells, 4 rest on the para-canonical Niddesa / Abhidhamma rather than the mula-Nikaya; the carita-as-temperament keying, the heart-blood-colour diagnostic, the teacher-diagnosis-then-misassignment machinery, and the saddha->recollection match are coded as commentarial innovations (H1). "Faithful in principle, innovative in apparatus."
h0_h1.framing_note: 8/7 is a definitional restatement of the warrant-present/absent coding, hard-pinned by build_dataset.py (regression gate); it is reported as a descriptive distribution, not a hypothesis test, and the warrant-tier calls are single-investigator (see verification_note).

meta.d0_verdict: The canon prescribes samatha and vipassanā YOKED / mutually conducive (AN 4.170 Yuganaddha; SN 43.2; AN 4.92-94/10.54 individualise only by the four-puggala TYPE, never assigning vipassanā alone to a named person), NOT as two separate vehicles. The samatha-yānika / vipassanā-yānika ("dry-insight", sukkhavipassaka) two-vehicle split is COMMENTARIAL (Vism / Vism-mhṭ / Abh-ṭ). Confirms Cousins.
meta.verification_note: Every instance grounded to a real passage id, fetched live. verification ∈ {verified (quote confirmed in the fetched passage), exists (passage fetched, quote not auto-matched, e.g. folded-diacritic or cross-row), exists-quote-unconfirmed, unresolved}.
meta.residual_gaps: Tool-load (not absence): verbatim Vism III object-rows not pulled at the leaf level (the work= search filter is unhonored); maraṇassati→named-person and MN 119 kāyagatāsati-as-directed-assignment unrun; the Mettasutta tree-deva nidāna unchased. See RESEARCH-DESIGN §6 + the census synthesis.

query_log.engine: PostgreSQL ~* (case-insensitive regex) over passages.original, run by direct SQL through the dhamma-pg proxy; the search service was not used for the frame. Every sql field below is a complete, runnable statement.
query_log.note: The four FRAME RULES define the candidate set: their deduplicated union is the 755 candidate passages, which were coded down to the 263 instances + 459 reasoned exclusions. Each rule is a single multi-term regex (the object rule alone carries about twenty terms). The other tables here are not part of building the census: the MEASUREMENT queries are the per-layer footprint counts that diagnosed the undercount the re-census corrects, and the TARGETED queries resolved specific cells and loci. Most individual canonical instances were then confirmed by direct fetch of the cited passage (reachable from every citation link), not by further search.

frame_rules.rule: Assignment narrative (commentary + sub-commentary + extra-canonical): a teacher gives a person a meditation subject
frame_rules.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug = 'pli-vism' OR work_slug LIKE '%-tika' OR work_slug LIKE 'pli-cst-%') AND original ~* 'kammaṭṭhān' AND original ~* 'adāsi|ācikkhi|kathesi|uggaṇhāpesi|uggaṇhi|ārocesi';

frame_rules.rule: Develop/attend directive paired with a named meditation object (any layer)
frame_rules.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE ( original ~* 'bhāvehi|bhāvetha|bhāveyyāsi|bhāveyyātha|bhāvetabb' OR original ~* 'manasi karohi|manasi karotha' ) AND original ~* 'asubh|mettā|mettaṃ|mettaṁ|ānāpān|kasiṇ|aniccasaññ|maraṇasati|maraṇassati|kāyagatā|paṭikūl|dhātumanasikār|buddhānussati|dhammānussati|saṅghānussati|cāgānussati|sīlānussati|devatānussati|catunnaṃ dhātūnaṃ|catunnaṁ dhātūnaṁ';
SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'rāgacaritassa|dosacaritassa|mohacaritassa|vitakkacaritassa|saddhācaritassa|buddhicaritassa|ñāṇacaritassa';

frame_rules.rule: Calm and insight co-treatment (sutta + para-canon + abhidhamma)
frame_rules.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE (work_slug NOT LIKE '%-attha' AND work_slug NOT LIKE '%-tika' AND work_slug <> 'pli-vism' AND work_slug NOT LIKE 'pli-cst-%' AND work_slug <> 'pli-vinaya') AND original ~* 'samath' AND original ~* 'vipassan';

measurement_queries.label: Object-term footprint (run per object across all layers; asubha shown)
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'asubh';

measurement_queries.label: Commentarial assignment idiom, all layers (to compare canon vs commentary)
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'kammaṭṭhān' AND original ~* 'adāsi|ācikkhi|kathesi|uggaṇhāpesi|uggaṇhi|ārocesi';

measurement_queries.label: Develop-imperative footprint (with and without an object)
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'bhāvehi|bhāvetha|bhāveyyāsi|bhāveyyātha|bhāvetabb';

measurement_queries.label: Carita footprint: any occurrence
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'carit';

measurement_queries.label: Carita footprint: temperament-typed forms
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'rāgacarit|dosacarit|mohacarit|saddhācarit|buddhicarit|vitakkacarit|ñāṇacarit';

measurement_queries.label: Calm + insight co-occurrence, all layers
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'samath' AND original ~* 'vipassan';

measurement_queries.label: Understanding-types footprint (ugghaṭitaññū / vipañcitaññū)
measurement_queries.sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE original ~* 'ugghaṭitaññ|vipañcitaññ|vipaccitaññ';

targeted_queries.label: Visuddhimagga III carita-to-object matrix
targeted_queries.sql: SELECT id, citation, original FROM passages WHERE work_slug = 'pli-vism' AND original ~* 'rāgacaritassa';

targeted_queries.label: Visuddhimagga III the forty objects (Cattālīsakammaṭṭhāna)
targeted_queries.sql: SELECT id, citation, original FROM passages WHERE work_slug = 'pli-vism' AND original ~* 'dasa kasiṇā|cattālīsa.{0,12}kammaṭṭhān';

targeted_queries.label: Mettā tree-deity origin story (Sn-a / Khp-a Mettasutta commentary)
targeted_queries.sql: SELECT id, citation, title FROM passages WHERE work_slug = 'pli-kn-attha' AND title ~* 'mettasutta' AND original ~* 'devatā|rukkh';

targeted_queries.label: Death-mindfulness directed to the bhikkhū (graded)
targeted_queries.sql: SELECT id, citation FROM passages WHERE work_slug IN ('pli-dn','pli-mn','pli-sn','pli-an','pli-kn','pli-dhp','pli-ud','pli-iti','pli-snp','pli-thag','pli-thig','pli-vv','pli-pv','pli-cp','pli-bv','pli-ja','pli-ap','pli-kp') AND original ~* 'maraṇasati|maraṇassati';

targeted_queries.label: Note: the F4 calm/insight cells and the four-element loci (AN 4.92-94, AN 9.4, MN 28/62/140, etc.) were confirmed by direct fetch of each cited passage, reachable from its citation link.

carita_sense_split.any_carita_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE work_slug IN ('pli-dn','pli-mn','pli-sn','pli-an','pli-kn','pli-dhp','pli-ud','pli-iti','pli-snp','pli-thag','pli-thig','pli-vv','pli-pv','pli-cp','pli-bv','pli-ja','pli-ap','pli-kp') AND original ~* 'carit';
carita_sense_split.temperament_compound_sql: SELECT id, work_slug FROM passages WHERE work_slug IN ('pli-dn','pli-mn','pli-sn','pli-an','pli-kn','pli-dhp','pli-ud','pli-iti','pli-snp','pli-thag','pli-thig','pli-vv','pli-pv','pli-cp','pli-bv','pli-ja','pli-ap','pli-kp') AND original ~* 'rāgacarit|dosacarit|mohacarit|saddhācarit|buddhicarit|vitakkacarit|ñāṇacarit|samacarit';
carita_sense_split.paṭisambhidāmagga_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM passages WHERE work_slug = 'pli-kn' AND id LIKE 'cst-s0517m%' AND original ~* 'rāgacarit|dosacarit|mohacarit';

query_log.integrity: Every coded instance evidence_pali was verified to be a literal substring of its source row (188/188 fine, 33/33 coarse, 9/9 added); 0 fabricated quotes.

meta.v2_note: v2.1 (provenance-retrofit R2): added the v2 block. The carita/kammaṭṭhāna/forty-object apparatus is coded by chronological stratum INDEPENDENTLY of structural layer (all post-canonical; 0 temperament-carita in the four Nikayas and 0 in the Abhidhamma). Carries the carita semantic-drift strip, the epistemic marking of the matching system (systematized, never asserted-verified; the Visuddhimagga's own na-sarato-paccetabbam disclaimer), and the cross-recensional split (seed pre-sectarian, typology Pali-local). The v1.2 three-tier counts and the samatha/vipassana-yoked verdict are unchanged (regression gate re-asserts them from the same census).

aggregates._aggregate_scope_note: by_layer/by_voice/by_mode/by_verification are scoped to the 55-row original census (their *_census55 siblings are identical); the 208 verified-expansion rows are counted only in the *_full263 siblings, by_tier, by_facet, and total. by_tier/by_facet/total are full-263. The live page recomputes every printed count from instances via filter, so these aggregates are a data convenience; the scope is named here so no consumer reads a 55-scoped count as 263-scoped.

instances.object: asubha; metta; anapanasati; aniccasanna (four-fold set)
instances.recipient: Venerable Meghiya (the Buddha's attendant)
instances.recipient_features: beset, while meditating in the mango grove, by three bad thoughts: kamavitakka, byapadavitakka, vihimsavitakka; went forth out of faith yet still harassed
instances.occasion: After Meghiya insists on going alone to the mango grove against the Buddha's advice and is overwhelmed by sensual/malicious/cruel thoughts, he returns and reports them
instances.evidence_pali: Tena ca pana, meghiya, bhikkhuna imesu pancasu dhammesu patitthaya cattaro dhamma uttari bhavetabba- asubha bhavetabba ragassa pahanaya, metta bhavetabba byapadassa pahanaya, anapanassati bhavetabba vitakkupacchedaya, aniccasanna bhavetabba asmimanasamugghataya. Aniccasannino, meghiya, anattasanna santhati. Anattasanni asmimanasamugghatam papunati dittheva dhamme nibbanan'ti.
instances.evidence_en: But then, a mendicant grounded on these five things should develop four further things. They should develop the perception of ugliness to give up greed, love to give up hate, mindfulness of breathing to cut off thinking, and perception of impermanence to uproot the conceit 'I am'. (Sujato)

instances.object: asubha; metta; anapanasati; aniccasanna (four-fold set)
instances.recipient: Venerable Meghiya
instances.recipient_features: same as AN 9.3 - overcome by kama/byapada/vihimsa-vitakka
instances.occasion: Udana version of the Meghiya episode (same narrative as AN 9.3)
instances.evidence_pali: asubha bhavetabba ragassa pahanaya, metta bhavetabba byapadassa pahanaya, anapanassati bhavetabba vitakkupacchedaya, aniccasanna bhavetabba asmimanasamugghataya
instances.evidence_en: They should develop the perception of ugliness to give up greed, love to give up hate, mindfulness of breathing to cut off thinking, and perception of impermanence to uproot the conceit 'I am'. (Sujato, parallel rendering)
instances.tr_provenance: sujato (corpus translation field of ud4.1; verbatim Pali via grep)

instances.object: asubha; metta; anapanasati; aniccasanna (four-fold set)
instances.recipient: the mendicants as a class (bhikkhave)
instances.recipient_features: general assembly; no individual trait stated - the de-personalised parallel of the Meghiya teaching
instances.occasion: Buddha at Savatthi addresses the bhikkhus on what ripens the heart's release (cetovimutti)
instances.evidence_pali: Tena ca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhuna imesu pancasu dhammesu patitthaya cattaro dhamma uttari bhavetabba- asubha bhavetabba ragassa pahanaya, metta bhavetabba byapadassa pahanaya, anapanassati bhavetabba vitakkupacchedaya, aniccasanna bhavetabba asmimanasamugghataya.
instances.evidence_en: Same four-fold formula as AN 9.3 but addressed to 'bhikkhave'. (Sujato)
instances.tr_provenance: sujato (corpus translation field) + verbatim Pali grep of an9.1

instances.object: anapanasati
instances.recipient: Venerable Rahula (taught by Sariputta)
instances.recipient_features: young monk sitting cross-legged at a tree-root having just been advised by the Buddha on the five aggregates as not-self
instances.occasion: Sariputta sees Rahula seated in meditation posture and tells him to develop anapanasati
instances.evidence_pali: Disvana ayasmantam rahulam amantesi: 'anapanassatim, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi. Anapanassati, rahula, bhavana bhavita bahulikata mahapphala hoti mahanisamsa'ti.
instances.evidence_en: Venerable Sariputta saw him sitting there, and addressed him, 'Rahula, develop mindfulness of breathing. When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it's very fruitful and beneficial.' (Sujato, segs 5.2-5.4)

instances.object: metta; karuna; mudita; upekkha; asubha; aniccasanna (object->defilement block)
instances.recipient: Venerable Rahula
instances.recipient_features: Buddha's son; given the full graded curriculum object by object
instances.occasion: Rahula asks how anapanasati is developed; the Buddha first walks him through the five elements ('meditate like the earth...'), then the brahmavihara-and-perception block before anapanasati proper
instances.evidence_pali: Mettam, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi. Mettanhi te, rahula, bhavanam bhavayato yo byapado so pahiyissati. Karunam, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi. Karunanhi te, rahula, bhavanam bhavayato ya vihesa sa pahiyissati. Muditam ... ya arati sa pahiyissati. Upekkham ... yo patigho so pahiyissati. Asubham, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi. Asubhanhi te, rahula, bhavanam bhavayato yo rago so pahiyissati. Aniccasannam ... yo asmimano so pahiyissati.
instances.evidence_en: Meditate on love. For when you meditate on love any ill will will be given up. Meditate on compassion ... cruelty ... rejoicing ... discontent ... equanimity ... aversion. Meditate on ugliness ... any lust will be given up. Meditate on impermanence ... the conceit 'I am'. (Sujato, segs 18-23)

instances.object: four/five/six element analysis (pathavi/apo/tejo/vayo/akasa) and 'meditate like the elements'
instances.recipient: Venerable Rahula
instances.recipient_features: instructed to become unshaken by pleasant/unpleasant contacts (manapamanapa phassa)
instances.occasion: Same teaching session; Buddha details each internal element, then 'pathavisamam bhavanam bhavehi...' so that pleasant/unpleasant contacts will not occupy the mind
instances.evidence_pali: Pathavisamam, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi. Pathavisamanhi te, rahula, bhavanam bhavayato uppanna manapamanapa phassa cittam na pariyadaya thassanti. ... Aposamam ... Tejosamam ... Vayosamam ... Akasasamam, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi.
instances.evidence_en: Rahula, meditate like the earth. For when you meditate like the earth, pleasant and unpleasant contacts will not occupy your mind. ... Meditate like water ... fire ... wind ... space. (Sujato, segs 13.1-17.5)

instances.object: anapanasati (sixteen-step elaboration)
instances.recipient: Venerable Rahula
instances.recipient_features: asked the Buddha directly how anapanasati is to be developed for great fruit
instances.occasion: Closing teaching: the Buddha lays out the sixteen steps of anapanasati for Rahula
instances.evidence_pali: Anapanassatim, rahula, bhavanam bhavehi. Anapanassati hi te, rahula, bhavita bahulikata mahapphala hoti mahanisamsa. ... Idha, rahula, bhikkhu arannagato va rukkhamulagato va sunnagaragato va nisidati pallankam abhujitva ... So satova assasati satova passasati.
instances.evidence_en: Develop mindfulness of breathing. When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it's very fruitful and beneficial. ... It's when a mendicant-gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut-sits down cross-legged ... Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. (Sujato, segs 24.1-30.2)

instances.object: ten perceptions: aniccasanna, anattasanna, asubhasanna, adinavasanna, pahanasanna, viragasanna, nirodhasanna, sabbaloke anabhiratasanna, sabbasankharesu anicchasanna, anapanasati
instances.recipient: Venerable Girimananda (relayed by Ananda)
instances.recipient_features: abadhiko dukkhito balhagilano - sick, suffering, gravely ill
instances.occasion: Ananda asks the Buddha to visit the gravely ill Girimananda; the Buddha instead has Ananda recite the ten perceptions, on hearing which the illness subsides on the spot
instances.evidence_pali: Sace kho tvam, ananda, girimanandassa bhikkhuno dasa sanna bhaseyyasi, thanam kho panetam vijjati yam girimanandassa bhikkhuno dasa sanna sutva so abadho thanaso patippassambheyya. Katama dasa? Aniccasanna, anattasanna, asubhasanna, adinavasanna, pahanasanna, viragasanna, nirodhasanna, sabbaloke anabhiratasanna, sabbasankharesu anicchasanna, anapanassati.
instances.evidence_en: Ananda, if you were to recite to the mendicant Girimananda these ten perceptions, it's possible that after hearing them his illness will die down on the spot. What ten? The perceptions of impermanence, not-self, ugliness, drawbacks, giving up, fading away, cessation, dissatisfaction with the whole world, impermanence of all conditions, and mindfulness of breathing. (Sujato, segs 2.3-3.2)

instances.object: asubha (vs raga); metta (vs dosa); panna (vs moha)
instances.recipient: the mendicants as a class (bhikkhave)
instances.recipient_features: no individual; explicit three-root object->defilement scheme
instances.occasion: Short doctrinal sutta in the Tikavagga: three things (raga, dosa, moha) and the three to develop to give them up
instances.evidence_pali: Imesam kho, bhikkhave, tinnam dhammanam pahanaya tayo dhamma bhavetabba. Katame tayo? Ragassa pahanaya asubha bhavetabba, dosassa pahanaya metta bhavetabba, mohassa pahanaya panna bhavetabba.
instances.evidence_en: To give up these three things you should develop three things. What three? You should develop the perception of ugliness to give up greed, love to give up hate, and wisdom to give up delusion. (Sujato, segs 1.5-1.7)

instances.object: anapanasatisamadhi (replacing asubhabhavana)
instances.recipient: the mendicant Sangha resident near Vesali (bhikkhusangha)
instances.recipient_features: had over-practised asubha to the point of revulsion at the body and mass suicide (10/20/30 monks per day sought the knife)
instances.occasion: After the Buddha's fortnight of praising asubha leads to the suicide crisis, Ananda asks for 'another method' (annam pariyayam); the Buddha gathers the Sangha and reassigns anapanasati as a peaceful, pleasant abiding that settles unskillful states
instances.evidence_pali: ayampi kho, bhikkhave, anapanassatisamadhi bhavito bahulikato santo ceva panito ca asecanako ca sukho ca viharo uppannuppanne ca papake akusale dhamme thanaso antaradhapeti vupasameti.
instances.evidence_en: Mendicants, when this immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it's peaceful and sublime, a delectable and blissful meditation. And it disperses and settles unskillful qualities on the spot whenever they arise. (Sujato, seg 6.3)

instances.object: asubha (vs the subha-nimitta tied to lust); kayagatasati; animitta (vs the mana-anusaya)
instances.recipient: Venerable Rahula
instances.recipient_features: the Buddha 'regularly' (abhinham) advised Rahula with these verses; addressed as a young monk to guard against the attractive sign
instances.occasion: Verse instruction (Sutta Nipata); after exhortations on restraint and good friends, the Buddha gives the object assignments in gatha form
instances.evidence_pali: Nimittam parivajjehi, subham ragupasanhitam; Asubhaya cittam bhavehi, ekaggam susamahitam. Animittanca bhavehi, Mananusayamujjaha; Tato manabhisamaya, Upasanto carissati'ti.
instances.evidence_en: Turn away from the sign that's attractive, provoking lust. With mind unified and serene, meditate on the ugly aspects of the body. Meditate on the signless, give up the tendency to conceit; and when you comprehend conceit, you will live at peace. (Sujato, segs 7.1-8.4)

instances.object: asubha (body-contemplation as diseased/filthy/rotten)
instances.recipient: Nanda (Sundarinanda therii)
instances.recipient_features: attached to / proud of bodily beauty (the verse names her 'nande' and points at 'this bag of bones'); reflecting on it she becomes disillusioned and quenched
instances.occasion: The Buddha addresses Nanda with the asubha verse; she then investigates the body inside and out and attains peace
instances.evidence_pali: Aturam asucim putim, passa nande samussayam; Asubhaya cittam bhavehi, ekaggam susamahitam. ... Atha nibbindaham kaye, ajjhattanca virajjaham; Appamatta visamyutta, upasantamhi nibbuta'ti.
instances.evidence_en: Nanda, see this bag of bones as diseased, filthy, and rotten. With mind unified and serene, meditate on the ugly aspects of the body ... Then, growing disillusioned with the body, I became dispassionate within. Diligent, detached, I'm quenched and at peace. (Sujato, segs 1.1-5.4)

instances.object: asubha (body-contemplation)
instances.recipient: Nanda (Abhirupananda therii)
instances.recipient_features: renowned for beauty (abhirupa), attached to her form
instances.occasion: Same asubha verse addressed to Nanda; the Dukanipata version
instances.evidence_pali: Aturam asucim putim, passa nande samussayam; Asubhaya cittam bhavehi, ekaggam susamahitam.
instances.evidence_en: Nanda, see this bag of bones as diseased, filthy, and rotten. With mind unified and serene, meditate on the ugly aspects of the body. (Sujato, parallel of THIG 5.4)
instances.tr_provenance: sujato (corpus translation field; verbatim Pali from search snippet of thig2.1)

instances.object: six-temperament -> object mapping: ragacarita->11-fold asubha; dosacarita->4-fold metta(-adi brahmavihara); mohacarita->maranassati(-adi); vitakkacarita->anapanasati/pathavi-kasina; saddhacarita->buddhanussati(-adi); buddhicarita->catudhatuvavatthana
instances.recipient: the body of monks taking up meditation subjects before the rains-retreat (sambahula nanaverajjaka bhikkhu)
instances.recipient_features: classified by the 84,000 carita-types; each receives the subject suited to his temperament
instances.occasion: Commentarial nidana to the Mettasutta (Khuddakapatha / Suttanipata commentary): the Buddha distributes kammatthanas by temperament
instances.evidence_pali: Tatra sudam bhagava ragacaritanam savinnanakavinnanakavasena ekadasavidham asubhakammatthanam, dosacaritanam catubbidham mettadikammatthanam, mohacaritanam maranassatikammatthanadini, vitakkacaritanam anapanassatipathavikasinadini, saddhacaritanam buddhanussatikammatthanadini, buddhicaritanam catudhatuvavatthanadiniti imina nayena caturasitisahassappabhedacaritanukulani kammatthanani katheti.
instances.evidence_en: The Blessed One taught meditation subjects suited to the 84,000 temperament-types thus: to the greed-natured the eleven-fold ugliness subject, to the hate-natured the four-fold loving-kindness subject and the like, to the delusion-natured mindfulness of death and the like, to the speculation-natured mindfulness of breathing and the earth-kasina and the like, to the faith-natured recollection of the Buddha and the like, to the intelligence-natured the defining of the four elements and the like. (author-gloss; no Sujato translation - commentary)
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (no published EN in corpus; Pali verbatim from cst-s0505a.att-10_p003, duplicated at cst-s0501a.att-99_p005)

instances.object: asubha-kammatthana + charnel-ground practice (susane carika)
instances.recipient: Venerable Kulla (Kullatthera)
instances.recipient_features: ragacaritatta tibbaragajatiko - greed-natured with fierce, recurring lust whose defilements repeatedly overwhelmed his mind
instances.occasion: The Buddha, knowing Kulla's mental conduct (cittacaram natva), gives him the asubha subject and tells him to walk often in the charnel ground; when lust still surges the Buddha conjures a decaying corpse-vision to fix the asubha perception
instances.evidence_pali: So ca ragacaritatta tibbaragajatiko hoti. Tenassa abhikkhanam kilesa cittam pariyadaya titthanti. Athassa sattha cittacaram natva asubhakammatthanam datva, 'kulla, taya abhinham susane carika caritabba'ti aha.
instances.evidence_en: And being greed-natured he was of a fierce-lust disposition, so his defilements repeatedly overwhelmed his mind. Then the Teacher, knowing the bent of his mind, gave him the meditation subject of ugliness and said, 'Kulla, you should often walk in the charnel ground.' (author-gloss; commentary on Theragatha Kulla verses)
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (no published EN; Pali verbatim from cst-s0508a2.att-31_p002)

instances.object: asubha-kammatthana
instances.recipient: an unnamed monk ('avuso mahabhuti')
instances.recipient_features: ragacarito - recognised by the elder as greed-natured
instances.occasion: Anguttara commentary (Nivaranappahanavagga): an elder, judging 'this one is greed-natured', teaches him the asubha subject; the monk then makes asubha the basis (padaka) for insight and attains arahatship by dawn
instances.evidence_pali: Thero 'ragacarito aya'nti natva asubhakammatthanam kathesi. ... channam sepannigacchamulam gantva pallankena nisinno asubhakammatthanam padakam katva vipassanam patthapetva arahatte patitthaya paccusakale uddesam sampapuni.
instances.evidence_en: The elder, knowing 'this one is greed-natured', taught the ugliness meditation subject. ... he sat cross-legged at the foot of the sepanni thicket, made the ugliness subject the basis, set up insight, and being established in arahatship reached the recitation at dawn. (author-gloss; commentary)
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (no published EN; Pali verbatim from cst-s0401a.att-an1_2_p030)

instances.object: asubha-kammatthana (and subjects by temperament)
instances.recipient: those of greed temperament (ragacarita) among the disciples
instances.recipient_features: classified by cariya (temperament)
instances.occasion: Digha commentary on the Dasuttarasutta: the Teacher gives by temperament - to the greed-natured the asubha subject
instances.evidence_pali: Sattha tesam cariyavasena ragacaritassa asubhakammatthanam deti.
instances.evidence_en: The Teacher gives by way of their temperament: to the greed-natured, the ugliness meditation subject. (author-gloss; commentary)
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (no published EN; Pali verbatim from cst-s0103a.att-dn3_11_p002)

instances.object: (mis-assigned) asubha-patikula-kammatthana, then REASSIGNED a pleasant red object - golden lotus / 'lohitakam lohitakam' (red-kasina parikamma)
instances.recipient: the goldsmith's-son monk (Suvannakara-bhikkhu, Sariputta's pupil)
instances.recipient_features: abhirupo young monk; Sariputta reasoned 'young men have excess lust' and gave asubha, but it was asappaya (unsuitable) - he could not gain even one-pointedness in three months; the Buddha saw by asaya-knowledge that he had been a goldsmith working red gold and lotuses for 500 lives
instances.occasion: After Sariputta's asubha assignment fails three times he takes the monk to the Buddha; the Buddha judges the repulsive subject unfit and a pleasant subject fit, conjures a psychic golden lotus, and assigns 'lohitakam, lohitakam' - the monk attains jhana at once
instances.evidence_pali: Thero 'tarunanam rago ussanno hoti'ti cintetva tassa ragapatighataya asubhakammatthanam adasi. Tassa pana tam asappayam. ... 'tasma imassa asubhapatikulakammatthanam na vattati, manapamevassa kammatthanam sappaya'nti cintetva ... iddhiya cakkamattam suvannapadumam mapetva ... 'bhikkhu imam padumam adaya ... lohitakam lohitaka'nti parikammam karohi'ti adasi.
instances.evidence_en: The elder [Sariputta], thinking 'young men have abundant lust', gave him the ugliness meditation subject to strike down lust. But it was unsuitable for him. ... [The Buddha, knowing he had been a goldsmith for 500 lives] thought 'therefore the repulsive ugliness subject does not suit him; only a pleasant subject is suitable for him', ... conjured by psychic power a wheel-sized golden lotus ... and said 'Monk, take this lotus ... and do the preparatory work: red, red.' (author-gloss; Dhammapada commentary, Suvannakara-thera story)
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (no published EN; Pali verbatim from cst-s0502a.att-234_p003 + cst-s0502a.att-234_p004)

instances.recipient: bhikkhus (doctrinal frame; the four puggalā are the typology objects)
instances.recipient_features: the four understanding-types named
instances.occasion: Puggalavagga doctrinal teaching on the four individuals found in the world
instances.evidence_pali: Cattārome, bhikkhave, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ. Katame cattāro? Ugghaṭitaññū, vipañcitaññū, neyyo, padaparamo-
instances.evidence_en: These four individuals are found in the world. What four? One who understands immediately, one who understands after detailed explanation, one who needs education, and one who merely learns by rote. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: the four puggalā (typology definitions, Catukkapuggalapaññatti)
instances.recipient_features: ugghaṭitaññū / vipañcitaññū / neyya / padaparama defined by HOW they awaken - i.e. by the teaching-mode each requires
instances.occasion: Puggalapaññatti §§148-151 four-type analysis
instances.evidence_pali: 148. ...ugghaṭitaññū? Yassa puggalassa saha udāhaṭavelāya dhammābhisamayo hoti... 149. ...vipañcitaññū? Yassa puggalassa saṃkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthe vibhajiyamāne dhammābhisamayo hoti... 150. ...neyyo? Yassa puggalassa uddesato paripucchato yoniso manasikaroto kalyāṇamitte sevato bhajato payirupāsato evaṃ anupubbena dhammābhisamayo hoti... 151. ...padaparamo? Yassa puggalassa bahumpi suṇato bahumpi bhaṇato bahumpi dhārayato bahumpi vācayato na tāya jātiyā dhammābhisamayo hoti-
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: §148 ugghaṭitaññū = one for whom realization of the Dhamma occurs together with the utterance; §149 vipañcitaññū = realization when the meaning of what is said in brief is analysed in detail; §150 neyya = realization gradually, through recitation, questioning, wise attention, and associating with good friends; §151 padaparama = no realization in this very life despite much hearing/reciting/retaining (Pp; corpus untranslated, author rendering)

instances.recipient: bhikkhus (the three gilānūpamā puggalā as objects of analysis)
instances.recipient_features: three receptivity-types: never-attains regardless; attains regardless; attains ONLY-IF taught (the conditional middle type)
instances.occasion: Puggalavagga simile of three patients explaining why dhammadesanā is prescribed at all
instances.evidence_pali: ...ekacco puggalo labhantova tathāgataṁ dassanāya no alabhanto, labhantova tathāgatappaveditaṁ dhammavinayaṁ savanāya no alabhanto okkamati niyāmaṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ... imaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalaṁ paṭicca dhammadesanā anuññātā.
instances.evidence_en: Some people can step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities, but only if they get to see a Realized One, and to hear the teaching... it’s for the sake of this last individual that teaching the Dhamma is prescribed. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: all beings surveyed before the first teaching (agency act)
instances.recipient_features: tikkhindriya/mudindriya (keen vs weak faculties), apparajakkha/mahārajakkha, suviññāpaya/duviññāpaya - the three-lotus survey
instances.occasion: After Brahmā Sahampati's appeal; the Buddha surveys the world with the buddhacakkhu to decide whether/whom to teach
instances.evidence_pali: Addasā kho bhagavā buddhacakkhunā lokaṁ volokento satte apparajakkhe mahārajakkhe tikkhindriye mudindriye svākāre dvākāre suviññāpaye duviññāpaye... Seyyathāpi nāma uppaliniyaṁ vā paduminiyaṁ vā puṇḍarīkiniyaṁ vā appekaccāni... udakā accuggamma ṭhitāni anupalittāni udakena;
instances.evidence_en: The Buddha saw sentient beings with little dust in their eyes, and some with much dust...; with keen faculties and with weak faculties...; easy to teach and hard to teach... Like a pool with lotuses, some grow underwater, some reach the surface, some rise clear of the water. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: all beings surveyed (parallel to SN 6.1) + named candidates Āḷāra Kālāma and Uddaka Rāmaputta
instances.recipient_features: tikkhindriya/mudindriya survey; Āḷāra & Uddaka assessed as 'apparajakkhajātiko' (long had little dust) - selection of first teaching-recipient by faculty
instances.occasion: Ariyapariyesanā: deciding whom to teach first after awakening
instances.evidence_pali: Addasaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhikkhave, buddhacakkhunā lokaṁ volokento satte apparajakkhe mahārajakkhe, tikkhindriye mudindriye, svākāre dvākāre, suviññāpaye duviññāpaye... ‘ayaṁ kho āḷāro kālāmo paṇḍito viyatto medhāvī dīgharattaṁ apparajakkhajātiko.’
instances.evidence_en: I saw sentient beings with keen faculties and with weak faculties...; ‘That Āḷāra Kālāma is astute, competent, clever, and has long had little dust in his eyes.’ (Sujato)

instances.recipient: Sāriputta (frame); the doctrinal object is the Tathāgata's knowledge of OTHER beings' faculties
instances.recipient_features: parasattānaṁ parapuggalānaṁ indriyaparopariyatta - the faculties of other beings, high and low
instances.occasion: Mahāsīhanāda: enumerating the ten Tathāgata-powers
instances.evidence_pali: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, tathāgato parasattānaṁ parapuggalānaṁ indriyaparopariyattaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti... idampi, sāriputta, tathāgatassa tathāgatabalaṁ hoti...
instances.evidence_en: Furthermore, the Realized One truly understands the faculties of other sentient beings and other individuals... this is a power of the Realized One. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: Bāhiya Dārucīriya
instances.recipient_features: ugghaṭitaññū exemplar - awakens on a single brief utterance; pressing urgency (asks thrice)
instances.occasion: Bāhiya intercepts the Buddha on alms-round and begs for teaching; Buddha gives one brief instruction standing
instances.evidence_pali: ‘diṭṭhe diṭṭhamattaṁ bhavissati, sute sutamattaṁ bhavissati, mute mutamattaṁ bhavissati, viññāte viññātamattaṁ bhavissatī’ti... Atha kho bāhiyassa dārucīriyassa bhagavato imāya saṅkhittāya dhammadesanāya tāvadeva anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimucci.
instances.evidence_en: ‘In the seen will be merely the seen; in the heard, merely the heard; in the thought, merely the thought; in the known, merely the known.’ ... Then, due to this brief Dhamma teaching, Bāhiya’s mind was right away freed from defilements by not grasping. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: Venerable Mālukyaputta
instances.recipient_features: vipañcitaññū exemplar - receives a brief verse, expands the meaning himself, then attains; elder (jiṇṇo vuddho mahallako, stated earlier in the sutta)
instances.occasion: Mālukyaputta asks for teaching in brief (saṅkhittena) so he can practise alone
instances.evidence_pali: “Ettha ca te, mālukyaputta... diṭṭhe diṭṭhamattaṁ bhavissati...” / “Imassa khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi...” / Atha kho āyasmā mālukyaputto... brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ... sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi. Aññataro ca panāyasmā mālukyaputto arahataṁ ahosīti.
instances.evidence_en: “In the seen will be merely the seen...” / “This is how I understand the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement...” / Then Māluṅkyaputta... soon realized the supreme end of the spiritual path... and became one of the perfected. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: Upāli the householder (former Nigaṇṭha lay follower)
instances.recipient_features: neyya-type: needs staged leading; mind brought to readiness (kallacitta) before the decisive teaching
instances.occasion: Upāli debates then is taught; the Buddha gauges his readiness mid-instruction
instances.evidence_pali: Atha kho bhagavā upālissa gahapatissa anupubbiṁ kathaṁ kathesi, seyyathidaṁ-dānakathaṁ sīlakathaṁ saggakathaṁ, kāmānaṁ ādīnavaṁ... nekkhamme ānisaṁsaṁ pakāsesi. Yadā bhagavā aññāsi upāliṁ gahapatiṁ kallacittaṁ muducittaṁ vinīvaraṇacittaṁ... atha yā buddhānaṁ sāmukkaṁsikā dhammadesanā taṁ pakāsesi... virajaṁ vītamalaṁ dhammacakkhuṁ udapādi.
instances.evidence_en: The Buddha taught Upāli step by step: giving, ethics, heaven, the drawbacks of sensual pleasures, the benefit of renunciation. When he knew Upāli’s mind was ready, pliable, rid of hindrances... he explained the special teaching of the Buddhas... the stainless, immaculate vision of the Dhamma arose. (Sujato)

instances.recipient: the three types as teaching-targets (Netti analysis; exemplified by Mogharāja, cited)
instances.recipient_features: ugghaṭitaññū / vipañcitaññū / neyya each receive a DIFFERENT content-set; further cross-cut by taṇhā/diṭṭhi-carita + manda/udatta faculty-strength
instances.occasion: Desanāhāravibhaṅga: how the Buddha varies the teaching by person-type
instances.evidence_pali: Tattha bhagavā ugghaṭitaññussa puggalassa nissaraṇaṁ desayati, vipañcitaññussa puggalassa ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca desayati, neyyassa puggalassa assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca desayati. Tattha catasso paṭipadā, cattāro puggalā. Taṇhācarito mando satindriyena dukkhāya paṭipadāya dandhābhiññāya niyyāti satipaṭṭhānehi nissayehi...
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: For the ugghaṭitaññū the Blessed One teaches escape only; for the vipañcitaññū, danger and escape; for the neyya, gratification, danger, and escape. There are four modes of practice for four persons: the craving-temperament dull-faculty one is led by the faculty of mindfulness via the painful-slow path, relying on the satipaṭṭhānas... (Netti; corpus untranslated, author rendering)

instances.recipient: the three types (Peṭakopadesa analysis)
instances.recipient_features: ugghaṭitaññū = tikkhindriya, vipañcitaññū = mudindriya, neyya = still-weaker faculties; each mapped to a sotāpanna grade (ekabījī / kolaṅkola / sattakkhattuparama)
instances.occasion: Hārasampātabhūmi: yutti-hāra correlating person-type, faculty, and fruit
instances.evidence_pali: Tividhā puggalā koci ugghaṭitaññū koci vipañcitaññū koci neyyo. Ugghaṭitaññū tikkhindriyo ca tato vipañcitaññū mudindriyo tato mudindriyehi neyyo. Tattha ugghaṭitaññū tikkhindriyatāya dassanabhūmimāgamma sotāpattiphalaṁ pāpuṇāti, ekabījako bhavati...
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: People are of three kinds: some ugghaṭitaññū, some vipañcitaññū, some neyya. The ugghaṭitaññū is keen-faculty, the vipañcitaññū weaker, the neyya weaker still. The keen-faculty one reaches the fruit of stream-entry and becomes a one-seeder... (Peṭakopadesa; corpus untranslated, author rendering)

instances.recipient: all beings as surveyed by the buddhacakkhu (Paṭisambhidāmagga Indriyakathā)
instances.recipient_features: tikkhindriya glossed = the faithful person (saddho puggalo); mudindriya = the faithless (assaddho) - analytic unpacking of MN 12/SN 6.1
instances.occasion: Indriyakathā: defining the buddhacakkhu = buddhañāṇa = indriyaparopariya-ñāṇa
instances.evidence_pali: Yaṁ buddhacakkhu taṁ buddhañāṇaṁ... yena cakkhunā tathāgato satte passati apparajakkhe mahārajakkhe tikkhindriye mudindriye... Tikkhindriye mudindriyeti saddho puggalo tikkhindriyo, assaddho puggalo mudindriyo …pe…
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: What is the buddha-eye is the buddha-knowledge... the eye by which the Tathāgata sees beings of little/much dust, keen/weak faculties... ‘keen/weak faculties’: the faithful person is keen-faculty, the faithless person is weak-faculty. (Paṭisambhidāmagga; corpus untranslated, author rendering)

instances.recipient: beings surveyed by the Buddha (sorted into six groups)
instances.recipient_features: the world-net of beings, sorted by temperament after the awakening, before the first teaching
instances.occasion: Brahmā's request; the Buddha surveys beings with the Buddha-eye and groups them by temperament before deciding to teach
instances.warrant: dn (Ayācana/Brahmāyācana narrative - mula has the survey but NOT the six-carita typology)
instances.evidence_pali: Tattha sabbepi abhabbapuggale pahāya bhabbapuggaleyeva ñāṇena pariggahetvā – ‘‘ettakā rāgacaritā, ettakā dosamohavitakkasaddhābuddhicaritā’’ti cha koṭṭhāse akāsi.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: Having set aside all the incapable persons and grasped with knowledge only the capable persons, he made six divisions: 'so many are of greed-temperament, so many of hate-, delusion-, speculation-, faith-, and intelligence-temperament.'
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (no Sujato/ATI English for this commentary row)

instances.object: asubha (greed) / mettā (hate) / uddesa-paripucchā+dhammassavana (delusion) / ānāpānassati (speculation) / pasādanīya suttas (faith) / deep anicca suttas (intelligence)
instances.recipient: meditators sent to the Teacher for a subject (yogāvacara)
instances.recipient_features: the six temperaments named explicitly: rāga-, dosa-, moha-, vitakka-, saddhā-, ñāṇa-carita
instances.occasion: the Teacher assigns each a meditation subject according to temperament (cariyavasena), then they go to a suitable dwelling and strive
instances.warrant: ud4.1 + an9.1 (the four-fold antidote formula covers the rāga/dosa/vitakka cells; moha→uddesa and saddhā/ñāṇa cells have no single canonical antidote list)
instances.evidence_pali: Satthā tesaṃ cariyavasena rāgacaritassa asubhakammaṭṭhānaṃ deti. Dosacaritassa mettākammaṭṭhānaṃ, mohacaritassa uddeso paripucchā – ‘kālena dhammassavanaṃ, kālena dhammasākacchā, idaṃ tuyhaṃ sappāya’nti ācikkhati. Vitakkacaritassa ānāpānassatikammaṭṭhānaṃ deti. Saddhācaritassa pasādanīyasuttante buddhasubodhiṃ dhammasudhammataṃ saṅghasuppaṭipattiñca pakāseti. Ñāṇacaritassa aniccatādipaṭisaṃyutte gambhīre suttante katheti.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: The Teacher, according to their temperament, gives the greed-temperament person the meditation-subject of foulness (asubha); to the hate-temperament person, the meditation-subject of loving-kindness; to the delusion-temperament person, recitation and questioning - 'timely listening to Dhamma, timely discussion of Dhamma, this is suitable for you' he declares; to the speculation-temperament person he gives the breath-mindfulness subject; to the faith-temperament person he reveals, in confidence-inspiring discourses, the Buddha's full awakening, the Dhamma's well-taughtness, the Saṅgha's good practice; to the knowledge-temperament person he expounds deep discourses connected with impermanence and so on.

instances.object: asubha (the ten foulnesses) + kāyagatāsati
instances.recipient: rāgacarita (greed-temperament) person
instances.recipient_features: greed-dominant; commentary notes in the strong sense tibbakileso = of acute defilement
instances.occasion: object-assignment by temperament-fit (sappāya)
instances.warrant: ud4.1 / an9.1 - 'asubhā bhāvetabbā rāgassa pahānāya' (H0: asubha↔greed is canonically warranted; BUT the ADDITION of kāyagatāsati to this cell is unwarranted as a greed-antidote → that sub-element counts toward H1)
instances.evidence_pali: asubhā bhāvetabbā rāgassa pahānāya [Ud 4.1 / AN 9.1] ;; rāgadosamohacaritānaṃ yathākkamaṃ asubha-mettā-paccayākārakathā rāgādivinayanato sappāyāti ayaṃ sāsanayutti [Nett-a]
instances.evidence_en: Sujato (Ud 4.1 / AN 9.1): 'They should develop the perception of ugliness to give up greed.' Author-gloss (Nett-a): 'For greed-, hate-, and delusion-temperaments respectively, the talk of foulness, loving-kindness, and the dependent-origination mode is suitable because it disciplines greed and the rest - this is the scriptural fit (sāsanayutti).'

instances.object: four brahmavihāra + four colour kasiṇa (nīla/pīta/lohita/odāta)
instances.recipient: dosacarita (hate-temperament) person
instances.recipient_features: hate/aversion-dominant
instances.occasion: object-assignment by temperament-fit; explicit asappāya warning against mismatch
instances.warrant: ud4.1 / an9.1 - 'mettā bhāvetabbā byāpādassa pahānāya' warrants mettā↔hate (H0); but the THREE other brahmavihāra (karuṇā/muditā/upekkhā) and the FOUR colour-kasiṇa assigned to hate have NO canonical dosa-antidote warrant → H1
instances.evidence_pali: mettā bhāvetabbā byāpādassa pahānāya [Ud 4.1 / AN 9.1] ;; suvaṇṇaṃ dosacaritavasena, dubbaṇṇaṃ rāgacaritavasena. Etesañhi etāni sappāyāni, sā ca nesaṃ sappāyatā vitthārato visuddhimagge cariyaniddese vuttā [Mp-a] ;; yadi rāgacaritassa mettācetovimuttiṃ deseyya, sā desanā na yujjati asappāyabhāvato [Nett-a]
instances.evidence_en: Sujato (Ud 4.1 / AN 9.1): 'They should develop love to give up hate.' Author-gloss (Mp-a): the beautiful(-coloured kasiṇa is taken) by way of the hate-temperament, the ugly(-coloured) by way of the greed-temperament; for these are suitable to them, and their suitability is told in detail in the Visuddhimagga, in the exposition of temperaments (cariyaniddesa). Author-gloss (Nett-a): if one were to teach the loving-kindness release to a greed-temperament person, that teaching does not fit, being unsuitable.

instances.citation: Vism cross-ref via Mp-a 18 §87; Sv-a 11 §2 (uddesa variant); Nett-a §11.26 (paccayākāra variant)
instances.object: ānāpānassati (Vism, delusion+speculation share it); commentary variants give moha→uddesa/paripucchā/dhammassavana (Sv-a) and moha→paccayākāra/dependent-origination (Nett-a)
instances.recipient: mohacarita (delusion-temperament) person
instances.recipient_features: delusion-dominant; sluggish, needs guidance/discussion
instances.occasion: object-assignment by temperament; the three sources DISAGREE on the object, exposing curation
instances.warrant: cnd19 (Cuḷaniddesa Mogharajamaṇavapucchaniddesa, the para-canonical carita-matrix; mohacaritassa to uddesa/recitation, verified live)
instances.evidence_pali: parittaṃ vitakkacaritavasena āgataṃ, appamāṇaṃ mohacaritavasena [Mp-a, kasiṇa-extent] ;; mohacaritassa uddeso paripucchā ... idaṃ tuyhaṃ sappāyanti ācikkhati [Sv-a] ;; rāgadosamohacaritānaṃ yathākkamaṃ asubha-mettā-paccayākārakathā ... sappāyā [Nett-a]
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: (Mp-a) the limited (kasiṇa) is given by way of the speculation-temperament, the measureless by way of the delusion-temperament; (Sv-a) for the delusion-temperament person, recitation and questioning... 'this is suitable for you'; (Nett-a) for greed-, hate-, delusion-temperaments respectively, the talk of foulness, loving-kindness, and dependent-origination is suitable.

instances.object: anapanasati
instances.recipient: vitakkacarita (speculation/discursive-thought temperament) person
instances.recipient_features: given to restless discursive thinking (vitakka)
instances.occasion: object-assignment by temperament-fit
instances.warrant: ud4.1 / an9.1 - 'ānāpānassati bhāvetabbā vitakkupacchedāya' (H0: this cell is the most exactly canonically warranted - the canon explicitly assigns breath-mindfulness as the antidote to vitakka)
instances.evidence_pali: ānāpānassati bhāvetabbā vitakkupacchedāya [Ud 4.1 / AN 9.1] ;; Vitakkacaritassa ānāpānassatikammaṭṭhānaṃ deti [Sv-a]
instances.evidence_en: Sujato (Ud 4.1 / AN 9.1): 'mindfulness of breathing to cut off thinking.' Author-gloss (Sv-a): 'to the speculation-temperament person he gives the breath-mindfulness meditation-subject.'

instances.object: cha anussati (the six recollections: buddha/dhamma/saṅgha/sīla/cāga/devatā)
instances.recipient: saddhācarita (faith-temperament) person
instances.recipient_features: faith-dominant
instances.occasion: object-assignment by temperament; canonically the six recollections are the dwelling of a faithful noble disciple
instances.evidence_pali: Idha, mahānāma, ariyasāvako tathāgataṁ anussarati: ‘itipi so bhagavā ...’ ... dhammasotaṁ samāpanno buddhānussatiṁ bhāveti [AN 6.10] ;; Saddhācaritassa pasādanīyasuttante buddhasubodhiṃ dhammasudhammataṃ saṅghasuppaṭipattiñca pakāseti [Sv-a]
instances.evidence_en: Sujato (AN 6.10): 'Firstly, a noble disciple recollects the Realized One: That Blessed One is... - they enter the stream of the teaching and develop the recollection of the Buddha.' Author-gloss (Sv-a): 'to the faith-temperament person he reveals, in confidence-inspiring discourses, the Buddha's awakening, the Dhamma's well-taughtness, the Saṅgha's good practice.'

instances.object: maraṇasati + upasamānussati + āhāre paṭikūlasaññā + catudhātuvavatthāna (four-element definition); Sv-a variant: deep anicca-suttas
instances.recipient: buddhicarita / ñāṇacarita (intelligence-temperament) person
instances.recipient_features: of keen understanding; given subtle/deep objects
instances.occasion: object-assignment by temperament-fit
instances.warrant: cnd19 (Cuḷaniddesa carita-matrix, ñaṇacaritassa to vipassana-nimitta, verified live; the canonical object loci AN 6.19 / MN 10 remain unverified under load)
instances.evidence_pali: Ñāṇacaritassa aniccatādipaṭisaṃyutte gambhīre suttante katheti [Sv-a]
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss (Sv-a): 'to the knowledge-temperament person he expounds deep discourses connected with impermanence and the like.'

instances.object: the heart-blood / kammaṭṭhāna nimitta colour read as a temperament-diagnostic (rāga=red, dosa=black, moha=dishwater-grey, vitakka=horse-gram-juice, saddhā=kaṇikāra-flower, paññā=clear)
instances.recipient: the meditator whose temperament is being diagnosed
instances.recipient_features: all six caritas, diagnosed by the colour of the heart-blood
instances.occasion: commentarial diagnostic apparatus supporting the matrix (how to READ a person's carita)
instances.evidence_pali: Taṃ panetaṃ rāgacaritassa rattaṃ hoti, dosacaritassa kāḷakaṃ, mohacaritassa maṃsadhovanudakasadisaṃ, vitakkacaritassa kulatthayūsavaṇṇaṃ, saddhācaritassa kaṇikārapupphavaṇṇaṃ, paññācaritassa acchaṃ vippasannaṃ anāvilaṃ paṇḍaraṃ.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: That (heart-blood) is, for the greed-temperament person, red; for the hate-temperament, black; for the delusion-temperament, like meat-washing water; for the speculation-temperament, the colour of horse-gram broth; for the faith-temperament, the colour of the kaṇikāra flower; for the wisdom-temperament, clear, bright, unturbid, pale.

instances.recipient: n/a (lexical/analytical definition)
instances.recipient_features: definition of the term cariyā itself
instances.occasion: Paṭisambhidāmagga commentary on cariyānānattañāṇa - defines cariyā as 'moving/coursing in an object'
instances.warrant: ps-paṭis (the Paṭisambhidāmagga cariyānānattañāṇa is a late-canonical / Khuddaka analytical basis; the temperament-typology proper remains commentarial)
instances.evidence_pali: Cariyānānattañāṇaniddese viññāṇacariyātiādīsu ārammaṇe caratīti cariyā ... Ñāṇameva cariyā, ñāṇena vā cariyā, ñāṇena vā carati ...
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: In the exposition of the knowledge of the diversity of conduct (cariyā), in 'consciousness-conduct' etc.: it courses in an object, thus cariyā... Knowledge itself is conduct, or conduct by knowledge, or one courses by knowledge.

instances.recipient: bhikkhus at Ghosita's monastery, Kosambi
instances.recipient_features: monks and nuns who declare arahantship; classified by which of four modes they used
instances.occasion: Ananda addressing the assembly on how all who declare arahantship in the Buddha's presence did so by one of four paths
instances.evidence_pali: Idha, avuso, bhikkhu samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhaveti. ... Puna caparam, avuso, bhikkhu vipassanapubbangamam samatham bhaveti. ... Puna caparam, avuso, bhikkhu samathavipassanam yuganaddham bhaveti. ... Puna caparam, avuso, bhikkhuno dhammuddhaccaviggahitam manasam hoti. ... Yo hi koci, avuso, bhikkhu va bhikkhuni va mama santike arahattappattim byakaroti, sabbo so imehi catuhi maggehi, etesam va annatarena.
instances.evidence_en: Take a mendicant who develops serenity before discernment... Another develops discernment before serenity... Another develops serenity and discernment in conjunction... Another mendicant's heart is seized by restlessness to realize the teaching... All of the monks and nuns who declare in my presence that they have attained perfection, did so by one or other of these four paths. (sujato)

instances.recipient: bhikkhus
instances.recipient_features: same fourfold as F4-01
instances.occasion: CST Patipadavagga copy of the Yuganaddha discourse
instances.evidence_pali: Idha, avuso, bhikkhu samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhaveti. Tassa samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhavayato maggo sanjayati.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss (CST original untranslated): Here a mendicant develops insight preceded by serenity; as he does so the path is born in him.

instances.recipient: bhikkhus
instances.recipient_features: the four-mode practitioners, analytically expanded
instances.occasion: Khuddaka-Nikaya analytical elaboration of the Yuganaddha fourfold, with a Suttantaniddesa defining each mode
instances.evidence_pali: Idhavuso, bhikkhu samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhaveti. ... Katham samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhaveti? Nekkhammavasena cittassa ekaggata avikkhepo samadhi. Tattha jate dhamme aniccato ... vipassana.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss (PS original untranslated): How does one develop insight preceded by serenity? By way of renunciation, unification of mind, non-distraction, is concentration. Therein the states that have arisen [are seen] as impermanent... that is insight.

instances.recipient: four person-types: (a) has ceto-samatha not vipassana, (b) has vipassana not samatha, (c) has neither, (d) has both
instances.recipient_features: classified by which faculty is attained / lacking
instances.occasion: the Buddha enumerating the four individuals and directing each to remedy what they lack by approaching one who has it
instances.evidence_pali: yvayam puggalo labhi ajjhattam cetosamathassa na labhi adhipannadhammavipassanaya, tena ... yvayam puggalo labhi adhipannadhammavipassanaya so upasankamitva evamassa vacaniyo: 'katham nu kho, avuso, sankhara datthabba? ...' ... So aparena samayena labhi ceva hoti ajjhattam cetosamathassa labhi ca adhipannadhammavipassanaya. ... yvayam puggalo labhi ceva ... labhi ca adhipannadhammavipassanaya, tena ... tesu ceva kusalesu dhammesu patitthaya uttari asavanam khayaya yogo karaniyo.
instances.evidence_en: As for the individual who has serenity but not discernment: they should approach someone who has discernment and ask 'how should conditions be seen, comprehended, discerned?' ... After some time they have both serenity and discernment. ... As for the individual who has both: grounded on those skillful qualities, they should practice meditation further to end the defilements. (sujato)

instances.recipient: a self-examining mendicant
instances.recipient_features: checks himself for presence/absence of ceto-samatha and adhipanna-dhamma-vipassana
instances.occasion: the Buddha teaching self-review of skillful qualities (mirror simile), directing the mendicant to establish the faculty he has and apply yoga to the one he lacks
instances.evidence_pali: Sace, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paccavekkhamano evam janati: 'labhimhi ajjhattam cetosamathassa, na labhi adhipannadhammavipassanaya'ti, tena ... bhikkhuna ajjhattam cetosamathe patitthaya adhipannadhammavipassanaya yogo karaniyo. ... 'labhimhi adhipannadhammavipassanaya, na labhi ajjhattam cetosamathassa'ti, tena ... adhipannadhammavipassanaya patitthaya ajjhattam cetosamathe yogo karaniyo.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss / cf. sujato (English of an10.54 directive clauses not in fetched payload; rendering follows AN 4.94 sujato vocabulary): If a mendicant on review knows 'I have internal serenity of heart but not the higher wisdom of discernment of principles', then grounded on internal serenity he should apply himself to the higher wisdom; if he knows 'I have discernment but not serenity', then grounded on discernment he should apply himself to internal serenity.
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (an10.54 directive clauses untranslated in payload; cf. sujato AN 4.94 vocabulary)

instances.recipient: bhikkhus
instances.recipient_features: none stated; generic assembly
instances.occasion: the Buddha teaching the unconditioned (asankhata = end of greed/hate/delusion) and the path leading to it
instances.evidence_pali: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, asankhatagamimaggo? Samatho ca vipassana ca. Ayam vuccati, bhikkhave, asankhatagamimaggo.
instances.evidence_en: And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned? Serenity and discernment. This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned. (sujato)

instances.recipient: bhikkhus
instances.recipient_features: none; peyyala addressed generically
instances.occasion: the Buddha naming the two dhammas to be developed for the direct-knowledge / full-understanding / abandoning of each defilement (raga, dosa, moha ... pamada)
instances.evidence_pali: Ragassa, bhikkhave, abhinnaya dve dhamma bhavetabba. Katame dve? Samatho ca vipassana ca. ... Ragassa, bhikkhave, pahanaya dve dhamma bhavetabba. Katame dve? Samatho ca vipassana ca.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss (CST original untranslated; cf. sujato AN two-things peyyala): Mendicants, two things should be developed for the direct knowledge of greed. What two? Serenity and discernment. ... for the giving up of greed... Serenity and discernment.
instances.tr_provenance: author-gloss (CST original untranslated; cf. sujato AN two-things peyyala)

instances.recipient: the samathayanika and the vipassanayanika practitioners (typed, not named)
instances.recipient_features: samathayanika = produces access/absorption samadhi first; vipassanayanika = does NOT produce that serenity
instances.occasion: Manorathapurani-tika glossing AN 4.170's first two modes as spoken 'by way of' the two vehicles
instances.evidence_pali: Dasame samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhavetiti idam samathayanikassa vasena vuttam. So hi pathamam upacarasamadhim va appanasamadhim va uppadeti, ayam samatho. ... Vipassanapubbangamam samatham bhavetiti idam pana vipassanayanikassa vasena vuttam. So tam vuttappakaram samatham asampadetva pancupadanakkhandhe aniccadihi vipassati.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: In the tenth [sutta], 'develops insight preceded by serenity' is said by way of the SERENITY-VEHICLE practitioner: he first produces access- or absorption-concentration, this is serenity... 'develops serenity preceded by insight' is said by way of the INSIGHT-VEHICLE practitioner: he, WITHOUT having produced the aforesaid serenity, contemplates the five aggregates of clinging as impermanent etc.

instances.recipient: practitioners typed by which faculty they gain naturally
instances.recipient_features: pakatiya vipassanalabhi = one who naturally gains insight
instances.occasion: Manorathapurani word-commentary on AN 4.170
instances.evidence_pali: Vipassanapubbangamanti vipassanam pubbangamam purecarikam katva samatham bhaveti, pakatiya vipassanalabhi vipassanaya thatva samadhim uppadetiti attho.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: 'Preceded by insight' means: having made insight the forerunner he develops serenity; the sense is that one who NATURALLY gains insight, standing on insight, produces concentration.

instances.object: four-element-definition (catudhatuvavatthana)
instances.recipient: the suddhavipassanayaniko (pure-insight-vehicle worker) or the samathayaniko
instances.recipient_features: pure-insight worker discerns the four elements directly, by way of the brief or detailed method
instances.occasion: Visuddhimagga's purification-of-view chapter describing how the dry-insight worker begins material discernment
instances.evidence_pali: Suddhavipassanayaniko pana ayameva va samathayaniko catudhatuvavatthane vuttanam tesam tesam dhatupariggahamukhanam annataramukhavasena sankhepato va vitthato va catasso dhatuyo parigganhati.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss (Vism is a post-canonical manual, layer coded anya): But the PURE-INSIGHT-VEHICLE worker, or else this same SERENITY-VEHICLE worker, discerns the four elements - briefly or in detail - by way of one or another of the doors of element-discernment stated in the four-element-definition.

instances.object: vipassana unmixed with serenity-development (the dry-insight vehicle itself)
instances.recipient: the suddhavipassanayanika
instances.recipient_features: proceeds samatham anissaya (not relying on serenity)
instances.occasion: Vism sub-commentary defining the term suddhavipassanayaniko
instances.evidence_pali: Tattha samathayanikassa samathamukhena vipassanabhiniveso, vipassanayanikassa pana samatham anissayati aha 'suddhavipassanayaniko'ti, samathabhavanaya amissitavipassanayanavati attho.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: Therein, for the SERENITY-VEHICLE worker the entry into insight is via serenity; but for the insight-vehicle worker, [the entry is] NOT RELYING ON SERENITY - hence he said 'pure-insight-vehicle worker', the meaning being: an insight-vehicle UNMIXED WITH serenity-development.

instances.recipient: the sukkhavipassaka (dry-insight worker) and the samathayanika
instances.recipient_features: neither has yet attained supramundane consciousness; both work the 81 mundane cittas
instances.occasion: Abhidhamma sub-commentary on purification-of-view, naming the dry-insight worker explicitly
instances.evidence_pali: Lokuttaracittani pana neva sukkhavipassakassa, na samathayanikassa pariggaham gacchanti anadhigatatta.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: But supramundane consciousnesses come to be grasped by neither the DRY-INSIGHT worker nor the serenity-vehicle worker, since they have not [yet] attained them.

instances.recipient: samathayanika vs 'the other' (the dry-insight worker)
instances.recipient_features: samathayanika uses access/absorption samadhi; the other uses momentary samadhi (khanikasamadhi)
instances.occasion: Vism sub-commentary distinguishing the kind of concentration each vehicle relies on
instances.evidence_pali: Samathayanikassa hi upacarappanappabhedam samadhim itarassa khanikasamadhim, ubhayesampi vimokkhamukhattayam vina na kadacipi lokuttaradhigamo sambhavati.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: For the serenity-vehicle worker [the basis is] concentration of the access-and-absorption kind, for the OTHER [the dry-insight worker] momentary concentration; yet for BOTH, without the three doors of liberation there is never any supramundane attainment.

instances.object: the four satipatthanas distributed to four practitioner-types
instances.recipient: samathayanika (dull / keen) and vipassanayanika (dull / keen)
instances.recipient_features: samathayanika-dull, samathayanika-keen, vipassanayanika-dull, vipassanayanika-keen
instances.occasion: Abhidhamma sub-commentary mapping the four foundations of mindfulness onto vehicle-x-faculty types
instances.evidence_pali: Samathayanikassa ca mandassa akicchena adhigantabbanimittam pathamam satipatthanam visuddhimaggo, tikkhassa dutiyam, vipassanayanikassapi mandassa tatiyam, tikkhassa catuttham visuddhimaggoti.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: For the DULL serenity-vehicle worker the first foundation [body], whose sign is attainable without difficulty, is the path of purification; for the KEEN one the second [feeling]; for the DULL insight-vehicle worker the third [mind]; for the KEEN one the fourth [dhammas].

instances.object: samatha-preceded vipassana (samathapubbangamam vipassanam)
instances.recipient: Sunita (the former outcaste corpse-/refuse-scavenger, pukkusa)
instances.recipient_features: low-caste flower-scavenger ordained by the Buddha with 'ehi bhikkhu'
instances.occasion: the Buddha, having ordained Sunita, instructs him in meditation; Sunita reaches arahantship in the last watch
instances.evidence_pali: Yatha mam ovaditi 'evam samathapubbangamam vipassanam bhavehi'ti yatha mam ovadi, tatha satthuno vacanam akasim patipajjim.
instances.evidence_en: author-gloss: 'As he instructed me' - i.e. 'Develop insight preceded by serenity thus' - as he instructed me, so I did the Teacher's word and practised.

instances.object: samatha + adhipaññādhammavipassanā (the missing factor)
instances.recipient: Venerable Nandaka
instances.recipient_features: teaching the bhikkhū; the four cases of one who has calm and/or insight
instances.occasion: Nandaka's discourse in the assembly hall on the factors a faithful, virtuous monk must complete
instances.evidence_pali: Saddho ca, nandaka, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca lābhī ca ajjhattaṁ cetosamādhissa, na lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; canonical calm/insight (cetosamatha/adhipannadhammavipassana) capacity typology.

instances.object: the missing one of cetosamatha / adhipaññādhammavipassanā
instances.recipient: four persons (one with calm not insight, insight not calm, neither, both)
instances.recipient_features: classified by which of calm/insight they possess
instances.occasion: the Buddha prescribes, for each type, to stand on what one has and work up the other
instances.evidence_pali: tena, bhikkhave, puggalena ajjhattaṁ cetosamathe patiṭṭhāya adhipaññādhammavipassanāya yogo karaṇīyo.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; Buddha's canonical prescription to develop the missing one of calm/insight.

instances.object: cetosamatha / adhipaññādhammavipassanā (the four-fold typology)
instances.recipient: four persons (puggalā) classified by calm/insight
instances.recipient_features: the bare four-type list
instances.occasion: the Buddha enumerates the four persons by their possession of calm and insight
instances.evidence_pali: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, na lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; the four-person calm/insight classification is a canonical formula.

instances.object: chadhātuvavatthāna (six-element analysis)
instances.recipient: Pukkusāti
instances.recipient_features: a clansman gone forth on faith in the Buddha sight-unseen; met in a potter's shed and not yet recognizing the Buddha
instances.occasion: the Buddha discerns Pukkusāti's readiness and teaches him the analysis into six elements (dhātuvibhaṅga)
instances.evidence_pali: Chayimā, bhikkhu, dhātuyo- pathavīdhātu, āpodhātu, tejodhātu, vāyodhātu, ākāsadhātu, viññāṇadhātu.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; six-element analysis (Dhatuvibhanga) taught to Pukkusati by the Buddha.

instances.object: catudhātuvavatthāna (four-element analysis, internal + external, seen as anicca)
instances.recipient: the bhikkhū (Sāriputta's audience)
instances.recipient_features: Sāriputta expounds the four great elements as a vehicle for nibbidā
instances.occasion: the Mahāhatthipadopama discourse, analysing each element ajjhattika/bāhira to ground dispassion
instances.evidence_pali: Evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya disvā pathavīdhātuyā nibbindati, pathavīdhātuyā cittaṁ virājeti.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; four-element analysis for nibbida, a canonical meditation subject.

instances.object: maraṇasati (mindfulness of death)
instances.recipient: the bhikkhū (individually graded by how each develops it)
instances.recipient_features: monks report differing intervals; the Buddha grades them dandha (slack) vs tikkha (keen)
instances.occasion: the Buddha asks the monks how they develop death-mindfulness and corrects them toward the breath-by-breath form
instances.evidence_pali: maraṇassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā amatogadhā amatapariyosānā.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; maranasati is a canonical subject keyed by the Buddha himself (prereg-named).

instances.object: maraṇasati (mindfulness of death)
instances.recipient: the bhikkhū (individually graded)
instances.recipient_features: eightfold variant of the graded death-mindfulness scene
instances.occasion: as AN 6.19, with finer interval gradations
instances.evidence_pali: maraṇassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā amatogadhā amatapariyosānā. Bhāvetha no tumhe, bhikkhave, maraṇassatin”ti.
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; maranasati canonically taught (prereg-named warrant AN 8.73).

instances.object: kāyagatāsati (mindfulness directed to the body - the 14 sections)
instances.recipient: the bhikkhū
instances.recipient_features: the full kāyagatāsati syllabus: breath, postures, parts, four elements, charnel, the four jhānas
instances.occasion: the Buddha extols and teaches mindfulness of the body, its great fruits and the satiāyatana/abhiññā it yields
instances.evidence_pali: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, kāyagatāsati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā? Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā
instances.warrant_basis: Mula sutta self-warranted; kayagatasati syllabus is the canonical body-mindfulness subject (prereg-named).

instances.object: mettā (as both protection and meditation subject)
instances.recipient: the forest-dwelling monks harassed by tree-devas
instances.recipient_features: a group who fled their rains-residence after the rukkhadevatā harassed them
instances.occasion: the Buddha sends them back to the same lodging and gives them the Mettasutta as paritta-and-kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: Sace pana devatāhi abhayaṃ icchatha, imaṃ parittaṃ uggaṇhatha, etañhi vo parittañca kammaṭṭhānañca bhavissatī’’ti imaṃ suttamabhāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Metta as protection-and-kammatthana for deva-harassed monks is the canonical Karaniyamettasutta frame itself.

instances.object: paṭikūlamanasikāra / dvattiṃsākāra (the body-parts kammaṭṭhāna of this niddesa)
instances.recipient: three classes of pupil-monk distinguished by learning-status / intent: one already learned in the subject, one wishing to learn while residing nearby, one who will learn and go elsewhere
instances.recipient_features: pakatiyā uggahitakammaṭṭhāno; santike vasitvā uggaṇhitukāmo; uggaṇhitvā aññattha gantukāmo
instances.occasion: a teacher imparting the kammaṭṭhāna (ācariyena kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathentena), who must teach it in three ways according to pupil-type
instances.evidence_pali: Ācariyena pana kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathentena tividhena kathetabbaṃ. Eko bhikkhu pakatiyā uggahitakammaṭṭhāno hoti. Tassa ekaṃ dve nisajjavāre sajjhāyaṃ kāretvā kathetabbaṃ. Eko santike vasitvā uggaṇhitukāmo hoti. Tassa āgatāgatavelāya kathetabbaṃ. Eko uggaṇhitvā aññattha gantukāmo hoti.
instances.warrant_basis: Object (patikulamanasikara) is canonical, but the keying is the commentarial three-fold teach-by-pupil-type diagnosis machinery; no canonical pairing.

instances.object: paṭikūlamanasikāra / dvattiṃsākāra (32 body-parts recitation, dvattisākārapāḷi)
instances.recipient: two elders (dve therā) who requested a kammaṭṭhāna from Malayavāsī-Mahādeva Thera
instances.recipient_features: learned in two or three nikāyas (dve tayo nikāyā paguṇā) yet receptive/reverent (padakkhiṇaggāhino); became sotāpanna only through the recitation
instances.occasion: the two elders, having asked the thera for a kammaṭṭhāna, were given the dvattiṃsākāra recitation to do for four months
instances.evidence_pali: Thero kira tehi kammaṭṭhānaṃ yācito ‘cattāro māse imaṃ evaṃ sajjhāyaṃ karothā’ti dvattisākārapāḷiṃ adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Dvattimsakara (32 parts) recitation assigned with no carita-typing; the canonical body-parts subject (DN 22 / MN 10 / MN 119).

instances.object: vaṇṇa-kasiṇa (color) - the pupil is redirected from the repulsiveness recitation to color when the parts appear by color
instances.recipient: a pupil-monk for whom, while reciting the 32 parts, the object appears by color (vaṇṇato upaṭṭhāti)
instances.recipient_features: had previously done preliminary work on the color-kasiṇa (pubbe vaṇṇakasiṇe parikammaṃ katapubbaṃ)
instances.occasion: the pupil reports to the ovādācariya that color (not repulsiveness) appears; the teacher must not mislead and assigns the suitable subject
instances.evidence_pali: ‘Sādhu, sappurisa, pubbe tayā vaṇṇakasiṇe parikammaṃ katapubbaṃ bhavissati. Etadeva kammaṭṭhānaṃ tuyhaṃ sappāyaṃ. Vaṇṇavaseneva sajjhāyaṃ karohī’ti vattabbo.
instances.warrant_basis: Vanna-kasina assigned by how the object appears + past habituation (sappaya-matching); commentarial diagnosis machinery, no canonical warrant.

instances.object: paṭikūlamanasikāra (repulsiveness of the 32 parts) - confirmed as the suitable subject when the parts appear as repulsive
instances.recipient: a pupil-monk for whom, while reciting, the object appears as repulsive (paṭikūlato upaṭṭhāti)
instances.recipient_features: had previously practiced paṭikūlamanasikāra (pubbe paṭikūlamanasikāre yogo kato)
instances.occasion: the pupil reports to the ovādācariya; the teacher confirms suitability and directs repulsiveness-recitation, which leads through first jhāna and vipassanā to arahatta
instances.evidence_pali: ‘Sādhu, sappurisa, pubbe tayā paṭikūlamanasikāre yogo kato bhavissati. Etadeva kammaṭṭhānaṃ tuyhaṃ sappāyaṃ. Paṭikūlavaseneva sajjhāyaṃ karohī’ti vattabbo.
instances.warrant_basis: Object is canonical (patikulamanasikara) but keyed by the teacher-confirms-by-appearance + prior-practice sappaya diagnosis, which is commentarial.

instances.object: dhātumanasikāra / catudhātuvavatthāna (element-analysis recitation) - assigned when the parts appear as elements
instances.recipient: a pupil-monk for whom, while reciting, the object appears by way of elements (dhātuto upaṭṭhāti, from the parallel context)
instances.recipient_features: had previously practiced dhātumanasikāra (pubbe dhātumanasikāre yogo kato)
instances.occasion: the pupil reports to the ovādācariya; the teacher confirms suitability and directs element-recitation
instances.evidence_pali: ‘Sādhu, sappurisa, pubbe tayā dhātumanasikāre yogo kato bhavissati. Etadeva kammaṭṭhānaṃ tuyhaṃ sappāyaṃ. Dhātuvaseneva sajjhāyaṃ karohī’ti vattabbo.
instances.warrant_basis: Dhatumanasikara assigned by appearance-diagnosis + past habituation (sappaya-matching); commentarial machinery, not a canonical pairing.

instances.object: asubha
instances.recipient: a sāmaṇera (novice) walking ahead of a young monk to fetch tooth-wood
instances.recipient_features: novice who saw an asubha object, attained first jhāna and three fruits, then led the elder to the same spot; commentary's point is sabhāga-sappāya (a man's suitable object is a male corpse, a woman's a female corpse)
instances.occasion: on the way to fetch tooth-wood the novice saw an asubha, attained jhāna and fruits, then directed the daharo monk to stand at the same spot and look; commentary draws the sappāya/sabhāga lesson
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘bhante, iminā tāva maggena gantvā mayā ṭhitokāse muhuttaṃ puratthābhimukho ṭhatvā olokethā’’ti āha. So tathā katvā tena pattavisesameva pāpuṇi. Evaṃ ekaṃ asubhaṃ dvinnaṃ janānaṃ atthāya jātaṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Asubha->raga is canonical, but the instance's point is the sabhaga sex-matching rule (male/female corpse), which the canon does not key; commentarial.

instances.object: mettā
instances.recipient: a person of hating temperament (dosacarita)
instances.recipient_features: dosacarita; byāpāda (ill-will) to be abandoned; mettā is sappāya for this class
instances.occasion: exegesis of the mettā-sahagata / mettā-padabhājanīya formula, stating this kammaṭṭhāna is suited to the dosacarita and which person mettā first attains absorption toward
instances.evidence_pali: yasmā idaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ dosacaritassa sappāyaṃ, tasmā yathārūpe puggale ayaṃ mettā appanaṃ pāpuṇāti
instances.warrant_basis: Metta<->byapada has a canonical warrant, but this instance keys metta to the standing dosacarita temperament type - the paradigm carita-typing H1.

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna (given to a dosacarita)
instances.recipient: a (formerly puthujjana) elder of hating temperament, instructed by the arahant Dhammadinna
instances.recipient_features: dosacarito ahosi; a samādhi-attainer who, frightened by his own conjured elephant, recognised he was still a worldling (puthujjanabhāva)
instances.occasion: the khīṇāsava Dhammadinna exposes the elder's residual fear, declares he came to be his support, and gives him a kammaṭṭhāna; the elder takes it and attains arahatta in three steps on the walkway
instances.evidence_pali: ‘Bhante, tumhākaṃ avassayo bhavissāmiccevāhaṃ āgato, mā cintayitthā’ti kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi. Thero kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā caṅkamaṃ āruyha tatiye padavāre aggaphalaṃ arahattaṃ pāpuṇi. Thero kira dosacarito ahosi.
instances.warrant_basis: Object unstated; assignment rests entirely on the dosacarita temperament-typing + Vism carita-suitability matrix - the paradigm commentarial innovation, no canonical defilement-keying nameable.

instances.object: amatā dhātu (directing the mind to the deathless element / nibbāna) after abandoning māna, uddhacca, kukkucca
instances.recipient: Venerable Anuruddha
instances.recipient_features: samāpatti-attainer with dibba-cakkhu in his first rains but mind not yet liberated; afflicted by māna, uddhacca, kukkucca
instances.occasion: Anuruddha reports to Sāriputta that despite energy and concentration his mind is not freed; Sāriputta diagnoses the three faults and instructs him to abandon them and direct the mind to the deathless element - thus giving the kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: Sādhu vatāyasmā anuruddho ime tayo dhamme pahāya ime tayo dhamme amanasikaritvā amatāya dhātuyā cittaṃ upasaṃharatū’’ti evamassa thero kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Direct gloss of the source sutta: Sāriputta's diagnosis of the three faults and the instruction to direct the mind to the deathless element is the canonical content of the Anuruddha-mahāvitakka-sutta.

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna (requested and given on the vipassanā-dhura)
instances.recipient: a monk who chose the vipassanā-dhura (insight-burden) over the gantha-dhura
instances.recipient_features: vattasampanna; judged the book-burden too heavy for himself; his faith is born of suffering (dukkhūpanisā saddhā)
instances.occasion: the monk asks which dhuras exist, declares he will fulfil the vipassanā-dhura and requests a kammaṭṭhāna; the elder, judging him duty-fulfilling, gives it
instances.evidence_pali: vipassanādhuraṃ pūressāmi kammaṭṭhānaṃ me dethā’’ti vanditvā nisīdi. Thero ‘‘vattasampanno bhikkhū’’ti vattasīse ṭhatvā tassa kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi. So kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā vipassanāya ca kammaṃ karoti
instances.warrant_basis: Object unstated, criterion 'capacity'; rests on the commentarial gantha-dhura/vipassanā-dhura topos (Dhp-a) with no canonical antidote pairing.

instances.object: the eight mahāpurisa-vitakkas (with arahatta inserted at the four points) plus assignment of a suitable dwelling (senāsana-sappāya)
instances.recipient: Venerable Anuruddha
instances.recipient_features: established in the four places where arahatta is fixed; the Buddha determines which dwelling is suitable for him
instances.occasion: the Buddha, having taught the kammaṭṭhāna setting arahatta into the four points, considers which dwelling will be suitable and directs Anuruddha to stay in that very place
instances.evidence_pali: Iti bhagavā catūsu ṭhānesu arahattaṃ pakkhipanto kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathetvā ‘‘katarasenāsane nu kho vasantassa kammaṭṭhānaṃ sappāyaṃ bhavissatī’’ti āvajjento ‘‘tasmiññeva vasantassā’’ti ñatvā tena hi tvaṃ, anuruddhātiādimāha.
