instances.warrant_basis: The eight mahāpurisa-vitakkas and the Buddha's direction of the suitable dwelling for Anuruddha are the canonical content of the source sutta itself.

instances.object: asubha (a light asubha-based kammaṭṭhāna after subha-contemplation of a conjured female form)
instances.recipient: a samāpatti-attaining elder (60 vassā) who, gazing at his own conjured 16-year-old female form, aroused lobha and recognised he was still a puthujjana, instructed by his pupil Dhammadinna
instances.recipient_features: long-standing samāpatti-attainer but still a puthujjana; lobha arose on viewing the conjured form
instances.occasion: Dhammadinna leads the elder to conjure a lotus-pond, lotus, and 16-year-old female form and attend to it as subha until lobha arises, then teaches a light asubha kammaṭṭhāna; the elder attains arahatta with paṭisambhidā
instances.evidence_pali: therassa asubhavasena sallahukaṃ katvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathetvā therassa okāsaṃ kātuṃ bahi nikkhanto. Suparimadditasaṅkhāro thero tasmiṃ divāṭṭhānato nikkhantamatteyeva saha paṭisambhidāhi arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Asubha keyed to aroused lobha/rāga is the Meghiya antidote formula (asubhā bhāvetabbā rāgassa pahānāya); canonical object paired with this defilement by the canon. Carita framing is decorative.

instances.object: allaasubha-bhāvanā (fresh/wet-corpse contemplation in the charnel ground)
instances.recipient: the striving monks (padhānakammikā bhikkhū) coming to see the Buddha
instances.recipient_features: strenuous-practice monks in whom lust was latent; the Buddha foreknew lobha would arise and sent them to the charnel ground before they could see him
instances.occasion: the Buddha, before they arrive, has Ānanda send the monks to the charnel ground to do fresh-asubha contemplation rather than grant an audience; they arouse lobha, gain saṃvega, and begin insight
instances.evidence_pali: ‘āmakasusānaṃ gantvā allaasubhabhāvanaṃ karothā’ti pahiṇeyyāsī’’ti. Thero tesaṃ āgatānaṃ satthārā kathitasāsanaṃ ārocesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Fresh-corpse asubha for monks with latent lust: asubha→rāga Meghiya keying; the sīvathikā charnel-ground contemplations are also canonical (DN 22 / MN 10).

instances.object: kasiṇa (kasiṇaparikamma) together with the held kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient: a monk doing building work (navakamma) for his preceptor
instances.recipient_features: engaged in new construction work; at risk of dropping his recitation-path and meditation subject
instances.occasion: the elder directs the monk, while doing the navakamma, not to abandon the uddesa-path, to keep attending to the kammaṭṭhāna, and from time to time to do the kasiṇa preliminary work
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘āvuso, navakammaṃ karonto uddesamaggañca mā vissajji, kammaṭṭhānañca manasi karohi, kālena ca kālaṃ kasiṇaparikammaṃ karohī’’ti
instances.warrant_basis: Criterion 'situation' (construction work); kasiṇa is a canonical subject but no canonical passage keys kasiṇa-parikamma to a work-context - the situation-keying rests on Vism IV–V.

instances.object: mettā (mis-assigned: told to direct mettā first toward a dear person - a negative exemplum)
instances.recipient: a lay attendant, a minister's son (amaccaputta upaṭṭhāka), asking the Dakkhiṇavihāra elder
instances.recipient_features: questioner asks toward whom mettā should first be directed; had a dear and pleasing wife; following the instruction he directed mettā at his wife and went mad - negative exemplar of mis-assignment
instances.occasion: the amaccaputta asks how to begin mettā; the elder, omitting the sabhāga/visabhāga distinction, wrongly answers 'a dear person (piyapuggale)', causing infatuation/madness - cited as the negative example of mis-assigning a kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: eko upaṭṭhāko amaccaputto upasaṅkamitvā ‘‘mettāyantena paṭhamaṃ kīdise puggale mettāyitabba’’nti pucchi. Thero sabhāgavisabhāgaṃ anācikkhitvā ‘‘piyapuggale’’ti āha.
instances.warrant_basis: Negative exemplum about the mettā recipient-ordering rule (sabhāga/visabhāga, not beginning with a dear person), a Vism IX commentarial elaboration with no canonical source.

instances.object: kabaḷīkārāhāra-pariggaha (apprehending material food as upādārūpa → the 23 derived materialities; rūpa-pariggaha vipassanā base)
instances.recipient: the elder Nandābhaya, sick at Potaliyavihāra, asking his younger brother the catunikāyika Tissatthera (of Koḷitavihāra) for a kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient_features: sick elder (roge samuṭṭhite); requests a 'light' (sallahuka) kammaṭṭhāna; himself supplies the rūpa-pariggaha object
instances.occasion: during illness Nandābhaya asks his younger brother for a light kammaṭṭhāna; the object (apprehending edible food as derived materiality) is affirmed/given, and the sick elder grasps derived matter and attains arahatta - the positive counter-example of correct assignment
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘āvuso, mayhaṃ sallahukaṃ katvā ekaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathehī’’ti. Kiṃ, bhante, aññena kammaṭṭhānena, kabaḷīkārāhāraṃ pariggaṇhituṃ vaṭṭatīti?
instances.warrant_basis: Apprehending edible food as upādārūpa / the 23 derived materialities is Abhidhamma/Vism XVIII rūpa-pariggaha, not the canonical āhāre paṭikūlasaññā; illness-keyed 'light' kammaṭṭhāna has no canonical warrant.

instances.object: buddhānussati (recollection of the Buddha; one of the six anussati)
instances.recipient: a stream-enterer (sotāpanna), via Mahānāma's question
instances.recipient_features: sotāpanna seeking a dwelling-practice (nissayavihāra); Mahānāma the Sakyan asks on the stream-enterer's behalf
instances.occasion: Mahānāma asks the Buddha what abiding/dwelling a stream-enterer should rely on; the Buddha answers with buddhānussati
instances.evidence_pali: Buddhānussatiṃ bhāvetīti buddhānussatikammaṭṭhānaṃ brūheti vaḍḍheti. Iminā nayena sabbattha attho veditabbo. Iti mahānāmo sotāpannassa nissayavihāraṃ pucchi. Satthāpissa tameva kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Buddhānussati taught to a stream-enterer via Mahānāma is the direct content of the Mahānāma-sutta (six recollections incl. buddhānussati).

instances.object: maraṇasati (mindfulness of death)
instances.recipient: the monks (bhikkhū) addressed by the Buddha at Nātika (Giñjakāvasatha)
instances.recipient_features: the assembly of monks instructed to develop mindfulness of death
instances.occasion: the Buddha at the Giñjakāvasatha urges the monks to develop maraṇasati; the commentary notes the sutta taught it as leading to arahatta
instances.evidence_pali: Maraṇassatinti maraṇassatikammaṭṭhānaṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Maraṇasati exhorted to the monks is the canonical subject of the Paṭhama-Maraṇassati-sutta being commented on.

instances.object: aniccasaññā (perception of impermanence; also dukkhasaññā etc., with nekkhammavitakka)
instances.recipient: the audience addressed in the sutta (those still entertaining kāmavitakka)
instances.recipient_features: hearers directed to abandon sensual thoughts and attend to perception of impermanence
instances.occasion: gloss of the directive to 'think thus' / 'attend thus' - abandon kāmavitakka, cultivate nekkhammavitakka and attend to aniccasaññā
instances.evidence_pali: Evaṃ manasi karothāti evaṃ aniccasaññameva, dukkhasaññādīsu vā aññataraṃ manasi karotha.
instances.warrant_basis: Gloss of the source sutta's own directive 'evaṃ manasi karotha' = attend to aniccasaññā/dukkhasaññā; the saññā-set is a canonical meditation object (Girimānanda), restating canonical content not adding an object.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra / asubha - the five body-parts ending in skin)
instances.recipient: a boy being ordained (the dāraka the Buddha ordered ordained - narrated as Cūḷapanthaka's vatthu)
instances.recipient_features: a young ordinand (dāraka)
instances.occasion: the Buddha ordered an alms-monk to ordain the boy; at the going-forth the elder explained the meditation subject and ordained him
instances.evidence_pali: Thero tassa tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā pabbājesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka (kesā-lomā-nakhā-dantā-taco) is the opening pentad of the 32-parts paṭikūlamanasikāra, a canonical satipaṭṭhāna body-contemplation (also Khp 3); criterion unstated, no temperament-typing.

instances.object: vīriya-samatha / balancing the faculties (indriyasamatā) with grasping the nimitta - the lute-string simile of even effort
instances.recipient: the elder Soṇa (Soṇa Koḷivisa)
instances.recipient_features: over-energetic (accāraddha-vīriya) - energy too taut, leading to restlessness then slackness; formerly skilled at the lute
instances.occasion: after the lute-string simile the Buddha corrects Soṇa's over-strung energy, sets the kammaṭṭhāna, and 'places him in arahantship'
instances.evidence_pali: taṃ samathanimittaṃ vipassanānimittaṃ magganimittaṃ phalanimittañca gaṇhāhi nibbattehīti evamassa satthā arahatte pakkhipitvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: The lute-string simile of balanced effort / indriya-samatā for over-energetic Soṇa is the canonical content of the Soṇa-sutta itself.

instances.object: mettā (mettājhāna made the basis/pādaka for vipassanā)
instances.recipient: the elder Subhūti
instances.recipient_features: newly ordained; became foremost of the araṇavihārī (peaceful dwellers) and the dakkhiṇeyya (alms-worthy)
instances.occasion: after ordination Subhūti has a kammaṭṭhāna recited, goes to the forest, develops vipassanā on a mettā-jhāna base, and attains arahatta
instances.evidence_pali: So upasampanno dve mātikā paguṇaṃ katvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā araññe samaṇadhammaṃ karonto vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā mettājhānaṃ pādakaṃ katvā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Mettā(-jhāna) developed and used as base for insight; mettā is a canonical meditation subject (Karaṇīyamettā; cf. AN 5.161 mettā-cetovimutti). No carita-typing or non-canonical object - the pādaka framing is commentarial dressing.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra / asubha - the five skin-pentad)
instances.recipient: the boy Revata (Khadiravaniya-Revata, Sāriputta's youngest brother)
instances.recipient_features: a young man who fled an arranged marriage to ordain; brother of Sāriputta
instances.occasion: the forest paṃsukūlika monks ordain him at Sāriputta's standing instruction, explain the meditation subject, then later set him to the kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā pabbājayiṃsu. Atha naṃ paripuṇṇavassaṃ upasampādetvā kammaṭṭhāne yojayiṃsu.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka given to Revata at ordination is the canonical 32-parts paṭikūlamanasikāra opening pentad (satipaṭṭhāna body-contemplation; Khp 3); no temperament-typing, object canonically warranted.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified subject) → developed into vipassanā
instances.recipient: the elder Soṇa Kuṭikaṇṇa (pupil of Mahākaccāna)
instances.recipient_features: newly fully-ordained pupil of Mahākaccāna; later recited the Suttanipāta
instances.occasion: after higher ordination Soṇa has a kammaṭṭhāna recited, develops insight, and attains arahatta
instances.evidence_pali: So upasampanno kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā arahattaṃ patvā
instances.warrant_basis: Stock vatthu formula 'kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā → vipassanā → arahatta'; object unnamed, no canonical antidote-keying.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra / asubha - the five skin-pentad)
instances.recipient: the boy Dabba (Dabba Mallaputta), age seven
instances.recipient_features: a seven-year-old ordinand of mature past merit (katābhinīhāra); attained the four fruits as his hair-locks fell at ordination
instances.occasion: the Buddha directs a monk to ordain the boy; the monk explains the tacapañcaka subject while shaving him
instances.evidence_pali: So thero satthu vacanaṃ sutvā dabbakumāraṃ pabbājento tacapañcakaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka (kesā-lomā-nakhā-dantā-taco) is the satipaṭṭhāna body-parts contemplation; canonical paṭikūlamanasikāra (DN 22 / MN 10, also Khp 3).

instances.object: middha-vinodana-kammaṭṭhāna (the drowsiness-dispelling practice: ālokasaññā / light-perception and the chain of eight antidotes)
instances.recipient: the elder (Mahāmoggallāna), nodding with drowsiness
instances.recipient_features: exhausted and nodding off (pacalāyamāna) after seven days of walking-meditation effort
instances.occasion: the Buddha sees the elder nodding at the end of the walkway and teaches the eight ways of dispelling torpor
instances.evidence_pali: Ettakena ṭhānena bhagavā therassa middhavinodanakammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: The eight drowsiness-antidotes incl. ālokasaññā given to nodding Moggallāna directly parallel the Pacalāyamāna-sutta (AN 7.61).

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified subject) taken in the Buddha's own presence → vipassanā
instances.recipient: the elder Upāli
instances.recipient_features: foremost of the vinaya-holders; took the subject directly from the Tathāgata
instances.occasion: Upāli takes a kammaṭṭhāna directly from the Buddha, develops insight, and attains arahatta
instances.evidence_pali: Thero kira tathāgatasseva santike kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Stock 'kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā → vipassanā → arahatta' vatthu; object unnamed, no canonical warrant.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified subject) recited by the Buddha; then redirected to develop both vipassanā- and gantha-dhura
instances.recipient: the elder Upāli
instances.recipient_features: requested to live in the forest; the Buddha judged he should stay near and fulfil both vipassanā-dhura and gantha-dhura
instances.occasion: after ordination Upāli has the Buddha recite a kammaṭṭhāna and asks leave for forest-dwelling; the Buddha redirects him to stay so both duties mature
instances.evidence_pali: So pabbajitvā upasampanno satthāraṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā ‘‘mayhaṃ, bhante, araññavāsaṃ anujānāthā’’ti āha.
instances.warrant_basis: Unnamed kammaṭṭhāna; the vipassanā-dhura / gantha-dhura distinction is itself a commentarial formula, no canonical object.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: Rādha, an aged brahmin newly ordained under Sāriputta
instances.recipient_features: mahallaka (old), driven out by his family, kicchalābhī (gets requisites with difficulty); the Buddha saw his upanissaya
instances.occasion: after Sāriputta ordains him on the Buddha's instruction and gives him favorable lodging and food, then takes him to a village dwelling
instances.evidence_pali: Rādhatthero senāsanasappāyañca bhojanasappāyañca labhitvā sāriputtattherassa santike kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā nacirasseva arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Unnamed kammaṭṭhāna at teacher's side; canonical Rādha material (SN 23) gives no assignment.

instances.object: dvattiṃsākāra-kammaṭṭhāna (the thirty-two parts of the body / paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: a person whose upanissaya the elder discerned (the future Citta the householder, in a past life)
instances.recipient_features: upanissaya-endowed (the elder saw his supporting condition for awakening)
instances.occasion: the elder, perceiving the recipient's upanissaya, teaches and gives him the thirty-two-parts subject
instances.evidence_pali: Thero tassa upanissayaṃ disvā dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Dvattiṃsākāra (thirty-two parts) is the canonical paṭikūlamanasikāra body-parts list (DN 22 / MN 10; kāyagatāsati MN 119).

instances.object: brahmavihāra (the four divine abidings)
instances.recipient: the monks addressed (those whom the Buddha promised would grow in 'bhoga')
instances.recipient_features: those wishing to increase in 'bhoga', here glossed as the all-pervading brahmavihāra-wealth
instances.occasion: the Buddha glosses his promise to increase them in bhoga as the brahmavihāra-wealth, directing those who wish to grow to develop the four divine abidings
instances.evidence_pali: Tasmā tumhehi bhogena vaḍḍhitukāmehi ime brahmavihārā bhāvetabbā.
instances.warrant_basis: The four brahmavihāra are a canonical meditation subject; brahmavihāra-bhāvanā warranted in the canon (DN 13 Tevijja; AN 11 Mettānisaṃsa).

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the skin-pentad) given at ordination, then a further kammaṭṭhāna assigned by the Buddha
instances.recipient: Subhadda the wanderer (paribbājaka), newly ordained - the Buddha's last direct disciple
instances.recipient_features: a paribbājaka, last personal disciple, just ordained by Ānanda
instances.occasion: the elder (Ānanda) ordains him giving the tacapañcaka subject; the Buddha then, after full ordination, declares the kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ kathetvā kesamassuṃ ohāretvā kāsāyāni vatthāni acchādāpetvā saraṇāni datvā bhagavato santikaṃ ānesi. Bhagavā upasampādetvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka recited at ordination = canonical body-parts contemplation (DN 22 / MN 10, Khp 3); the further unnamed subject does not undercut the named canonical object.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna for abandoning the defilements destroyed by the three higher paths (uparimaggattayavajjha-kilesa); subject unnamed
instances.recipient: the (five hundred) monks, already stream-enterers (sotāpanna)
instances.recipient_features: already sotāpannas; the Buddha discerned their dissatisfaction (anabhirati) and gives them the subject for the three higher paths
instances.occasion: the Buddha seats the monks and announces he will teach a kammaṭṭhāna for abandoning the defilements still to be cut by the three higher paths
instances.evidence_pali: uparimaggattayavajjhānaṃ vo kilesānaṃ pahānāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathessāmī’’ti kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi
instances.warrant_basis: Unnamed kammaṭṭhāna keyed to defilements of the three higher paths; staged-path assignment is commentarial, no named object or canonical antidote-formula.

instances.object: aniccasaññā (and dukkhasaññā etc.); right thought (nekkhamma-vitakka) over wrong (kāma-vitakka)
instances.recipient: the disciples addressed in the anusāsanī-pāṭihāriya (a general 'you' instructed how to think and attend)
instances.recipient_features: none stated
instances.occasion: gloss of the Kevaṭṭa anusāsanī-pāṭihāriya ('think thus, attend thus'): cultivate perception of impermanence, abandon the fivefold sense-desire lust, attain the lokuttara-dhamma
instances.evidence_pali: Evaṃ manasi karothāti evaṃ aniccasaññameva, dukkhasaññādīsu vā aññataraṃ manasi karotha. Mā evanti ‘‘nicca’’ntiādinā nayena mā manasi karittha. Idanti idaṃ pañcakāmaguṇikarāgaṃ pajahatha.
instances.warrant_basis: Gloss of the anusāsanī-pāṭihāriya it glosses (DN 11 Kevaṭṭa); aniccasaññā and abandoning kāma-rāga via right attention are canonical subjects (AN 7.49; nekkhamma-vitakka MN 19/20).

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna (given through to arahatship; vipassanādhura)
instances.recipient: Cakkhupāla thera, ordained late in life, who elected the insight-burden
instances.recipient_features: mahallaka (ordained old); cannot fulfil the book-burden (ganthadhura), chooses the insight-burden (vipassanādhura)
instances.occasion: after 5 rains he asks the Buddha about the two burdens, elects vipassanādhura, and requests a meditation subject
instances.evidence_pali: vipassanādhuraṃ pana pūressāmi, kammaṭṭhānaṃ me kathethā’’ti. Athassa satthā yāva arahattaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Unnamed kammaṭṭhāna 'to arahatship'; ganthadhura/vipassanādhura framing is commentarial, no named canonical object.

instances.object: asubhakammaṭṭhāna (assigned first by Sāriputta), then lohitakakammaṭṭhāna (red-colour/blood object) assigned by the Buddha, leading to aniccasaññā
instances.recipient: a young monk, son of a goldsmith, ordained under Sāriputta
instances.recipient_features: young (dahara); a goldsmith for 500 lives; asubha unsuitable, suited to a red/blood object; buddhaveneyya (only the Buddha could discern his suitable object)
instances.occasion: after 4 fruitless months of the asubha Sāriputta gave, Sāriputta brings him to the Buddha, who discerns the suitable object and creates an iddhi-lotus
instances.evidence_pali: Tenassa ‘‘lohitakanimittaṃ sappāya’’nti ñatvā bhagavā lohitakakammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Carita/sappāya-suitability machinery (asubha unsuitable, Buddha substitutes lohitaka-nimitta via indriyaparopariyatti); the red/blood object is not a canonical meditation subject. Vism III suitability matrix is commentarial.

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna (leading to the jhānas)
instances.recipient: the 44,000 pupil-ascetics (antevāsika) of Nanda the ascetic
instances.recipient_features: resident pupils/students of the ascetic Nandamāṇava; became jhāna-attainers shortly after
instances.occasion: Nanda the ascetic, having attained the eight attainments and five abhiññā, teaches a meditation subject to his pupils (a former-life / pre-Buddha frame)
instances.evidence_pali: Antevāsikānampi kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi. Tepi na cireneva jhānalābhino ahesuṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Generic pre-Buddha teacher–pupil kammaṭṭhāna transmission, object unspecified; no canonical warrant.

instances.object: buddhānussati
instances.recipient: the second-person addressee of the verse (imperative bhāvehi; in Subhūti's apadāna frame a past Buddha directs the recipient)
instances.recipient_features: none stated in this fragment
instances.occasion: a verse directive to cultivate recollection of the Buddha
instances.evidence_pali: Bhāvehi buddhānussatiṃ, bhāvanānamanuttaraṃ; Imaṃ satiṃ bhāvayitvā, pūrayissasi mānasaṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Buddhānussati is the first of the canonical six recollections (cha-anussati; AN 6.10 Mahānāma).

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna, practised as mettājhāna-based vipassanā
instances.recipient: Subhūti (Subhūtikuṭumbika), newly ordained
instances.recipient_features: gained faith hearing the Dhamma with Anāthapiṇḍika; ordained; mastered two mātikā; later foremost of the peaceful-dwellers (araṇavihārī)
instances.occasion: after ordination he has a meditation subject expounded (causative kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā, assigner unnamed) and develops mettā-jhāna-based insight in the forest to arahatship
instances.evidence_pali: So upasampajjitvā dve mātikā paguṇā katvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā araññe samaṇadhammaṃ karonto mettājhānapādakaṃ vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Assignment proper is an unnamed kammaṭṭhāna (causative kathāpetvā, teacher unnamed); the mettā-jhāna-as-vipassanā-base is a retrospective practice description, itself commentarial (Vism IX), not a keyed canonical assignment.

instances.object: unstated kammaṭṭhāna (given through to arahatship; vipassanādhura) paired with the sosānika (charnel-ground) dhutaṅga
instances.recipient: Mahākāḷa thera, ordained late in life, who elected the insight-burden
instances.recipient_features: mahallaka (ordained old); cannot fulfil the book-burden, chooses the insight-burden; takes up the charnel-ground ascetic practice
instances.occasion: after upasampadā he asks the Buddha about the two burdens, elects vipassanādhura, has a meditation subject expounded to arahatship, then takes up the sosānika dhutaṅga
instances.evidence_pali: vipassanādhuraṃ pana pūressāmī’’ti yāva arahattā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā sosānikadhutaṅgaṃ samādāya
instances.warrant_basis: Object is unstated ('yāva arahattā kammaṭṭhānaṃ'); the ganthadhura/vipassanādhura division and the dhutaṅga keying are commentarial, with no canonical object-criterion pairing.

instances.object: asubha (contemplation of the body as diseased, impure, putrid)
instances.recipient: Nandā (Abhirūpanandā / Sundarī-Nandā), the nun addressed by name in the verse
instances.recipient_features: named (passa nande); rāga rooted in pride/conceit over her own beauty (abhirūpa)
instances.occasion: the Buddha's verse directing her to look at the body as foul and develop the mind toward asubha, one-pointed and well-composed
instances.evidence_pali: Āturaṃ asuciṃ pūtiṃ, passa nande samussayaṃ; Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: asubha → rāga/beauty-conceit is the canonical Meghiya antidote (asubhā rāgassa pahānāya); the foul-body verse to Nandā is itself canonical (Thīg).

instances.object: tacapañcaka (paṭikūlamanasikāra - the skin-pentad: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco)
instances.recipient: Dabbakumāra, a seven-year-old boy, at his going-forth
instances.recipient_features: pubbahetusampanno katābhinīhāro satto (endowed with prior cause, having made the resolve); ordained at age seven
instances.occasion: At his pabbajjā: the Buddha signals a monk to ordain him, and that monk, on the Buddha's word, gives the tacapañcaka subject while going him forth.
instances.evidence_pali: So thero satthu vacanaṃ sutvā dabbakumāraṃ pabbājento tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: tacapañcaka (kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco) is the canonical paṭikūlamanasikāra of the satipaṭṭhāna body-contemplation; standard pabbajjā object, no carita typing.

instances.object: mettā (mettā-cetovimutti / the four brahmavihāras - mettā karuṇā muditā upekkhā)
instances.recipient: the monks (bhikkhū), a class - monks generally
instances.recipient_features: none stated
instances.occasion: At Jetavana, apropos the Mettasutta, the Buddha addresses the bhikkhus, recites the eleven benefits of mettā-cetovimutti (AN 11.15), and directs that a monk should develop mettā toward all beings, designated and undesignated, and likewise karuṇā, muditā, upekkhā.
instances.evidence_pali: bhikkhave, bhikkhunā nāma sabbasattesu odissakānodissakavasena mettā bhāvetabbā, hitopi hitena pharitabbo
instances.warrant_basis: mettā / brahmavihāra-bhāvanā directed to monks is canonical (Karaṇīyamettasutta Snp 1.8; AN 11.15 benefits quoted verbatim).

instances.object: mettā and the four brahmavihāras (mettā karuṇā muditā upekkhā)
instances.recipient: the company of seers (isigaṇa), a class - the pupils of the teacher Araka (the bodhisatta)
instances.recipient_features: renunciant seers (pabbajita) under teacher Araka; a large following
instances.occasion: In a past life the bodhisatta, as the teacher Araka, master of the four brahmavihāras, exhorts his band of seers that a renunciant should develop mettā and the brahmavihāras, since the mettā-mind reaching absorption leads to the brahma-world.
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘pabbajitena nāma mettā bhāvetabbā, karuṇā muditā upekkhā bhāvetabbā. Mettacittañhi nāmetaṃ appanāppattaṃ brahmalokaparāyaṇataṃ sādhetī’’ti
instances.warrant_basis: brahmavihāra-bhāvanā culminating in brahmaloka is a canonical pairing (mettā/brahmavihāra → brahmaloka, cf. DN 13; Snp 1.8); object canonical though directed to past-life seers.

instances.object: caritānukūla kammaṭṭhāna - a meditation subject suited to his temperament (the specific subject is not named)
instances.recipient: Pilindavaccha, a former gandhāra-vijjā (mantra) master, newly gone forth
instances.recipient_features: upanissayasampanna (endowed with the supporting condition); ordained thinking the going-forth was preliminary to learning the Buddha's 'spell'
instances.occasion: After the Buddha teaches him Dhamma at his going-forth, the Buddha gives him a temperament-suited kammaṭṭhāna; through his supporting condition he soon develops insight and reaches arahatship.
instances.evidence_pali: Tassa bhagavā dhammaṃ kathetvā caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Explicit carita-temperament typing ('caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi'), object unnamed; the temperament-matching machinery is the commentarial innovation (rests on Vism III only).

instances.object: caritānukūla kammaṭṭhāna - a temperament-suited subject taken up for development (specific subject unnamed)
instances.recipient: Cūḷavaccha, a brahmin youth of Kosambī, newly gone forth
instances.recipient_features: paṭiladdhasaddho (having gained faith); accomplished in the brahmin arts; newly ordained, preliminary duties done
instances.occasion: After the Buddha teaches him Dhamma and he goes forth and is fully ordained, he takes up a temperament-suited kammaṭṭhāna and dwells developing it; later, staying in the Buddha's instruction amid the Kosambī monks' quarrel, he develops insight to arahatship.
instances.evidence_pali: So paṭiladdhasaddho pabbajitvā laddhūpasampado katapubbakicco caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā bhāvento vihari.
instances.warrant_basis: Carita-temperament typing ('caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā'), object unnamed; no canonical object-criterion warrant (Vism III matrix only).

instances.object: ajjhāsayānurūpa kammaṭṭhāna - a meditation subject suited to his disposition (specific subject unnamed)
instances.recipient: a sāmaṇera (young novice), pupil of the elder Saṃkicca (here the petas' nephew)
instances.recipient_features: sañjātasaṃvego - stirred to urgency by the encounter with his peta-relatives; resolved to delight in the holy life, no longer wanting household life
instances.occasion: After the saṃvega-rousing encounter with the petas, the novice falls at his preceptor's feet and renounces household life; the elder Saṃkicca then declares to him a disposition-suited kammaṭṭhāna, applying which he soon reaches arahatship.
instances.evidence_pali: Athāyasmā saṃkicco tassa ajjhāsayānurūpaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Disposition-reading / matching machinery ('ajjhāsayānurūpaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi'), object unnamed; the ajjhāsaya-diagnosis assignment is commentarial.

instances.object: caritānukūla kammaṭṭhāna (temperament-suited subject), then vipassanā method
instances.recipient: Sumana, ordained by his maternal uncle (an arahant living in the forest)
instances.recipient_features: young man of a Kosala merchant family, newly ordained by his arahant uncle once he came of age
instances.occasion: after Sumana came of age, his uncle (an arahant) ordained him and gave him a subject
instances.evidence_pali: taṃ pabbājetvā caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi. So tattha yogakammaṃ karonto cattāri jhānāni pañca ca abhiññāyo nibbattesi. Athassa thero vipassanāvidhiṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Carita-temperament typing ('caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi'), object unnamed; the temperament-matching scheme is the commentarial innovation (Vism III).

instances.object: aṭṭhikasaññā / aṭṭhikasaññā-kammaṭṭhāna (perception of the skeleton/bones)
instances.recipient: Siṅgālaka's father (Siṅgālapitā thera), newly ordained monk
instances.recipient_features: of a Sāvatthī family; had cultivated aṭṭhikasaññā in a former life under Kassapa Buddha (latent disposition); left household and ordained
instances.occasion: after he ordained, the Buddha surveyed his disposition (ajjhāsaya) and assigned the subject
instances.evidence_pali: Tassa bhagavā ajjhāsayaṃ olokento aṭṭhikasaññākammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: aṭṭhikasaññā is a named canonical meditation subject (Aṭṭhikasaññāsutta, SN 46.57); the ajjhāsaya-glance is the framing, but the object itself is canonically warranted.

instances.object: vedanāpariggaha kammaṭṭhāna (comprehension/grasping of feeling)
instances.recipient: Sāmāvatī and her ~500 women (upāsikās), trapped in the burning palace
instances.recipient_features: lay-women devotees of varied attainment (some already sotāpanna/sakadāgāmī), being burned alive in the palace
instances.occasion: as Māgaṇḍiyā's plot sets the palace ablaze, Sāmāvatī gives them an ovāda to stay heedful
instances.evidence_pali: Tā gehe jhāyante vedanāpariggahakammaṭṭhānaṃ manasikarontiyo kāci dutiyaphalaṃ, kāci tatiyaphalaṃ pāpuṇiṃsu.
instances.warrant_basis: vedanāpariggaha = vedanānupassanā of the satipaṭṭhāna scheme (DN 22 / MN 10), a standing canonical contemplation of feeling; object canonical despite the 'situation' framing.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna 'purified' and re-given (object unnamed); insight subject corrected because excess faith blocked vipassanā
instances.recipient: Vakkali thera, dwelling on Gijjhakūṭa
instances.recipient_features: his saddhā was so strong that vipassanā would not enter its course; later afflicted by a wind-disease from lack of food
instances.occasion: the Buddha, having admonished him ('kiṃ te vakkalī'), knew his insight was stuck and purified and re-gave the subject
instances.evidence_pali: tassa saddhāya balavabhāvato eva vipassanā vīthiṃ na otarati, bhagavā taṃ ñatvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Object unnamed ('kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā adāsi'); the faith-vs-wisdom indriya-balancing rationale for re-assigning a subject is commentarial diagnostic machinery (Vism IV).

instances.object: tacapañcaka kammaṭṭhāna (the five with skin: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco), paṭikūlamanasikāra
instances.recipient: the boy being ordained (Mahāpanthaka per coders 1 & 2; Cūḷapanthaka per coder 3) - the young grandson of the merchant
instances.recipient_features: a young grandson of a treasurer, inclined to going forth, being ordained
instances.occasion: a piṇḍapāta-going monk, ordered by the Buddha to ordain the boy, teaches the standard tacapañcaka at ordination
instances.evidence_pali: Thero tassa tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā pabbājesi.
instances.warrant_basis: tacapañcaka given at ordination is the canonical paṭikūlamanasikāra of the satipaṭṭhāna body-contemplation; standard ordinand object, no carita typing.

instances.object: cariyānukūla kammaṭṭhāna (conduct/temperament-suited subject), then vipassanā
instances.recipient: Gosāla thera, of a wealthy Magadhan (ibbha) family
instances.recipient_features: a man of great wealth, stirred to saṃvega on hearing of Soṇa Koṭikaṇṇa's going-forth; ordained under the Buddha
instances.occasion: after ordaining in the Buddha's presence, he took up a conduct-suited subject and sought a suitable dwelling
instances.evidence_pali: bhagavato santike pabbajitvā cariyānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā sappāyaṃ vasanaṭṭhānaṃ gavesanto
instances.warrant_basis: Carita-temperament typing ('cariyānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā'), object unnamed; temperament-matching is the commentarial innovation (Vism III).

instances.object: dvattiṃsākāra kammaṭṭhāna (32 body-parts) with paṭikūlamanasikāra, then the insight-path (vipassanāmagga)
instances.recipient: the brahmin's daughter (later Kesakārī devatā), a lay-woman
instances.recipient_features: discerning (viññū / viññubhāva), endowed with supporting condition (upanissayasampannā); had just taken refuge and precepts and asked what further to do
instances.occasion: a lay devotee (āgataphala upāsaka), perceiving her capacity, expounds the 32 parts and points out the insight-path
instances.evidence_pali: sarīrasabhāvaṃ vibhāvento dvattiṃsākārakammaṭṭhānaṃ kathetvā kāye virāgaṃ uppādetvā upari aniccatādipaṭisaṃyuttāya dhammiyā kathāya saṃvejetvā vipassanāmaggaṃ ācikkhitvā gato.
instances.warrant_basis: dvattiṃsākāra (32 parts) with paṭikūlamanasikāra is the canonical kāyagatāsati body-contemplation (MN 119 / DN 22); named canonical object.

instances.object: self-chosen kammaṭṭhāna (cittaruci; subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: a Sāvatthī clansman gone forth, a five-rains monk of slackened energy (ossaṭṭhavīriya bhikkhu)
instances.recipient_features: learned two mātikā, trained in vipassanā practice; chose his subject by his own inclination, later let his energy go slack
instances.occasion: after ordaining he takes a subject of his own choosing from the Teacher, enters a forest for the rains, and struggles three months without raising even a sign
instances.evidence_pali: satthu santike attano cittaruciyaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā ekaṃ araññaṃ pavisitvā vassaṃ upagantvā temāsaṃ vāyamanto
instances.warrant_basis: Self-chosen subject by inclination (cittaruci), unspecified; no named object and no canonical object-criterion pairing to warrant the assignment.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna up to arahantship (subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: about sixty monks (saṭṭhimattā bhikkhū)
instances.recipient_features: a band of monks setting out for a rains-residence at Mātikagāma
instances.occasion: the sixty monks have the Teacher state/expound a meditation subject 'up to arahantship' before going to the village for alms
instances.evidence_pali: saṭṭhimattā bhikkhū satthu santike yāva arahattā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā taṃ gāmaṃ gantvā piṇḍāya pavisiṃsu
instances.warrant_basis: Subject unspecified ('kammaṭṭhāna up to arahantship'); stock ordination-narrative, no named canonical object or defilement-keying.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna taken from his preceptor (subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: the elder Soṇa Kuṭikaṇṇa
instances.recipient_features: a newly ordained monk, pupil of his preceptor Mahākaccāna
instances.occasion: after ordination he takes a meditation subject from his own preceptor, develops insight, and attains arahantship (per the Aṅguttara-aṭṭhakathā)
instances.evidence_pali: upasampanno hutvā attano upajjhāyassa santike kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā arahattaṃ pāpuṇī
instances.warrant_basis: Unspecified subject taken from preceptor; no named canonical object or situation pairing.

instances.object: mettā (the Metta-sutta itself, made the pādaka for insight)
instances.recipient: the band of forest-dwelling monks (te bhikkhū)
instances.recipient_features: monks who had been frightened by hostile non-human beings (amanussā) in their forest dwelling
instances.occasion: after teaching the Metta-sutta the Buddha sends them back to that very grove to practise just this subject; their fear will not return
instances.evidence_pali: idameva kammaṭṭhānaṃ āsevatha, bhāvetha, bahulīkarotha
instances.warrant_basis: Mettā sent back to the forest against fear of non-human beings - the Karaṇīyamettasutta scenario itself; object and situation-keying both canonical.

instances.object: guarding/restraining one's own mind (cittaṃ rakkha / niggaṇha)
instances.recipient: a certain monk (aññataro bhikkhu)
instances.recipient_features: a worldling unsettled by a mind-reading laywoman at Mātikagāma, afraid of his own stray thoughts
instances.occasion: the monk flees the village fearing the laywoman who knows his thoughts; the Buddha sends him back with one thing to guard - his own mind - then speaks the verse
instances.evidence_pali: Tava cittameva rakkha, cittaṃ nāmetaṃ durakkhaṃ, tvaṃ attano cittameva niggaṇha, mā aññaṃ kiñci cintayi
instances.warrant_basis: 'Guard your own mind' is not a named canonical kammaṭṭhāna keyed to this situation; Dhp 33-37 describe the mind but prescribe no meditation-subject pairing here.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the skin-pentad; paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: the boy Mahāpanthaka (ordinand)
instances.recipient_features: a young grandson of the merchant, inclined to the going-forth
instances.occasion: the Buddha orders an alms-going monk to ordain the boy; the elder declares the tacapañcaka subject to him and ordains him
instances.evidence_pali: Thero tassa tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā pabbājesi
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka (kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco) is the body-parts/paṭikūlamanasikāra contemplation, a canonical meditation subject.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: the community of monks (bhikkhusaṅgha)
instances.recipient_features: the assembled saṅgha at the vihāra
instances.occasion: after the customary duties and the sugata-admonition, the Buddha teaches a meditation subject and dismisses the saṅgha
instances.evidence_pali: bhikkhusaṅghassa sugatovādaṃ datvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathetvā bhikkhusaṅghaṃ uyyojetvā
instances.warrant_basis: Unspecified subject taught to the saṅgha then dismissed; stock, no named object.

instances.object: mettā (mettābhāvanā / mettākammaṭṭhāna)
instances.recipient: the seven-year-old boy Sopāka (newly ordained)
instances.recipient_features: a young veneyya boy drawn by past merit, ordained by the Buddha with his father's consent
instances.occasion: the Buddha goes to the charnel-ground, teaches the boy, ordains him, and enjoins him in the cultivation of loving-kindness; he develops mettā-jhāna as the basis for insight and attains arahantship
instances.evidence_pali: Satthā taṃ pabbājetvā mettābhāvanāya niyojesi. So mettākammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā susāne viharanto
instances.warrant_basis: Mettābhāvanā enjoined; mettā is a canonical meditation subject (Karaṇīyamettasutta).

instances.object: mettā toward the four royal snake-families (catūsu ahirājakulesu mettaṃ)
instances.recipient: the company of ascetics/seers (tāpasā / isigaṇa)
instances.recipient_features: forest seers on the Ganges bank being killed by snakes and other long creatures
instances.occasion: the Bodhisatta-teacher assembles the dying ascetics and directs them to cultivate mettā toward the four snake-king families so the snakes will not bite them, then speaks the verse
instances.evidence_pali: sace tumhe catūsu ahirājakulesu mettaṃ bhāveyyātha, na vo sappā ḍaṃseyyuṃ, tasmā ito paṭṭhāya catūsu ahirājakulesu evaṃ mettaṃ bhāvethā
instances.warrant_basis: Mettā toward the four royal snake-families against snakebite - the canonical Ahirāja/Khandha-paritta formula; object and situation-keying both canonical.

instances.object: cariyānukūla kammaṭṭhāna (a subject suited to temperament; subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: the elder Sāmaññakāni
instances.recipient_features: son of a wanderer (paribbājaka), ordained after seeing the Twin Miracle
instances.occasion: upon going forth he takes up a meditation subject suited to his temperament, produces jhāna, makes it the basis for insight, and attains arahantship
instances.evidence_pali: sāsane pabbajitvā cariyānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā jhānaṃ nibbattetvā
instances.warrant_basis: 'cariyānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ' is the carita-temperament-matching machinery (Vism-only basis); subject unspecified; commentarial innovation.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna purified/clarified by the Buddha (subject unspecified)
instances.recipient: the elder Kumāputta (Nanda, son of Kumā)
instances.recipient_features: ordained under Sāriputta; unable to produce distinction (visesa) while practising the recluse-duty, so he approached the Buddha
instances.occasion: unable to win distinction at the mountain-side, he approaches the Buddha, hears Dhamma, has his meditation subject purified/corrected, dwells in a suitable place, and develops insight to arahantship
instances.evidence_pali: bhagavantaṃ upasaṅkamitvā dhammaṃ sutvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ sodhetvā sappāyaṭṭhāne vasanto vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā arahattaṃ sacchākāsi
instances.warrant_basis: Subject purified by the Buddha but unspecified; stock insight-to-arahantship narrative, no named canonical pairing.

instances.object: arūpakammaṭṭhāna (formless subject - nāma-rūpa contemplated by way of vedanā)
instances.recipient: those who awaken by way of feeling (vedanāvasena bujjhanakā)
instances.recipient_features: disposed (ajjhāsaya) to awaken via feeling / the formless subject
instances.occasion: Buddha teaching the Vedanāsutta to a class disposed to awaken via feeling
instances.evidence_pali: Idha pana bhagavā vedanāvasena bujjhanakānaṃ ajjhāsayena arūpakammaṭṭhānaṃ kathento vedanāvasena kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Load-bearing move is capacity-typing (ajjhāsaya 'those who awaken via feeling') matched to arūpakammaṭṭhāna; canon does not key the formless object to this standing disposition. Vedanānupassanā exists but the diagnosis-and-matching is commentarial.

instances.object: other (defilement-corruptions / āhāra-based upakkilesā mapped to carita-types, implying the carita-matched antidote objects)
instances.recipient: persons of lustful / aversive / view temperament (rāgacarita, dosacarita, diṭṭhicarita - dull and keen)
instances.recipient_features: rāgacarita, dosacarita, diṭṭhicarita (manda/tikkha)
instances.occasion: Niddesa exegesis correlating the four-quarter dhammas to the defilements of each temperament-type
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘ime rāgacaritassa puggalassa upakkilesā’’ti. Yathā ca paṭhamadisābhāvena vuttadhammā rāgacaritassa upakkilesā, evaṃ dutiyadisābhāvena vuttadhammā dosacaritassa.
instances.warrant_basis: Niddesa carita-matrix exegesis sorting upakkilesā into rāga/dosa/diṭṭhi temperament types - the core carita-typing innovation.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified subject)
instances.recipient: Khadiravaniya Revata (newly ordained, younger brother of Sāriputta)
instances.recipient_features: newly fully-ordained monk (20 years complete), saṃvega-driven, ripe in faculties
instances.occasion: after full ordination the monks established him in a meditation subject; he took it to the Khadira forest
instances.evidence_pali: Bhikkhū taṃ pabbājetvā paripuṇṇavīsativassaṃ upasampādetvā kammaṭṭhāne niyojesuṃ. So kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā khadiravanaṃ pavisitvā
instances.warrant_basis: Unspecified kammaṭṭhāna established at ordination; stock narrative, no named object.

instances.object: asubha (foulness of the body)
instances.recipient: Sundarī-Nandā (the therī), addressed by name ('Nande')
instances.recipient_features: attached to her own beauty/body (rāga; samussaya)
instances.occasion: verse instruction: 'Look, Nandā, at this ailing, impure, foul aggregate-body; develop the mind on the foul'
instances.evidence_pali: Āturaṃ asuciṃ pūtiṃ, passa nande samussayaṃ; Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Asubha given to one attached to her own beauty - the canonical asubha→rāga antidote (Meghiya/Girimānanda formula); object and defilement-keying both canonical.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified subject)
instances.recipient: Sumaṅgala (a poor farmer who ordained)
instances.recipient_features: former poor cultivator, ordained out of saṃvega, later ripe in knowledge
instances.occasion: a great elder, out of compassion, ordained him and explained a meditation subject
instances.evidence_pali: So taṃ karuṇāyanto pabbājetvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Unspecified kammaṭṭhāna explained at ordination; stock 'ācikkhi kammaṭṭhānaṃ' narrative.

instances.object: asubha (foulness of the body)
instances.recipient: Sundarī-Nandā (the therī), addressed by name ('Nande')
instances.recipient_features: attached to her own beauty/body (rāga; samussaya)
instances.occasion: verse instruction (variant): 'Look, Nandā, at this ailing, corpse-like, foul aggregate-body; develop the mind on the foul'
instances.evidence_pali: Āturaṃ kuṇapaṃ pūtiṃ, passa nande samussayaṃ; Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Variant verse, same assignment: asubha for attachment to own body (rāga) - canonical asubha→rāga antidote.

instances.object: asubha (asubhakammaṭṭhāna)
instances.recipient: a monk, formerly a goldsmith, pupil (saddhivihārika) of the Dhammasenāpati Sāriputta
instances.recipient_features: ex-goldsmith with 500 prior births in goldsmith families, accustomed to pure gold, so the foul was unsuitable for him
instances.occasion: Sāriputta, lacking the knowledge-of-dispositions (āsayānusaya-ñāṇa), assigned him only the foul subject, which proved unsuitable - he could not even raise the nimitta
instances.evidence_pali: dhammasenāpati attano āsayānusayañāṇassa natthitāya saddhivihārikassa āsayānusayaṃ ajānanto asubhakammaṭṭhānameva kathesi, tassa taṃ na sappāyamahosi.
instances.warrant_basis: Driven by the carita/asaya teacher-diagnosis machinery (asayanusaya-nana reading temperament); asubha judged unsuitable for a raga-clean ex-goldsmith - no canonical object-criterion pairing warrants this.

instances.object: asubha (asubhakammaṭṭhāna); reassigned by the Buddha to a lotus-flower object to gaze upon
instances.recipient: Sāriputta's saddhivihārika (co-resident pupil monk; buddhaveneyyo, a goldsmith's son in the Jātaka frame)
instances.recipient_features: buddhaveneyya (trainable only by the Buddha); produced not even a nimitta in four months under asubha
instances.occasion: Sāriputta cannot bring the pupil to arahatta and leads him to the Buddha, who diagnoses the temperament-mismatch and personally reassigns a lotus
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘bhikkhu imaṃ pupphaṃ olokento nisīdā’’ti nisīdāpetvā
instances.warrant_basis: Buddha reassigns lotus-gazing on a temperament-mismatch diagnosis; lotus-for-ex-goldsmith is not a canonical meditation-subject keying - pure commentarial diagnostic innovation.

instances.object: brahmavihāra-bhāvanā (mettā, karuṇā, muditā, upekkhā)
instances.recipient: the māṇava (a young brahmin youth)
instances.recipient_features: bhīto (frightened); had turned his mind back, begged pardon, and was reassured
instances.occasion: after the teacher reassures the youth, he directs him to develop the four divine abidings; the youth then re-attains jhāna and abhiññā
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘ehi tvaṃ, māṇava, mettaṃ bhāvehi, karuṇaṃ, muditaṃ, upekkha’’nti brahmavihārabhāvanaṃ ācikkhi
instances.warrant_basis: canonical brahmavihāra subject, DN 13 cultivation formula; no temperament-typing

instances.object: maraṇasati (with aniccasaññā / contemplation of saṅkhārā as impermanent)
instances.recipient: the Bodhisatta's five household members (wife, son, daughter, daughter-in-law, servant/dāsī) - a lay family class
instances.recipient_features: a harmonious brahmin farming family living together in concord; lay practitioners
instances.occasion: the Bodhisatta, as householder, regularly gives standing ovāda to the five members of his household
instances.evidence_pali: maraṇassatiṃ bhāvetha, tumhākaṃ maraṇabhāvaṃ sallakkhetha, imesañhi sattānaṃ maraṇaṃ dhuvaṃ, jīvitaṃ addhuvaṃ, sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā khayavayadhamminova
instances.warrant_basis: Maranasati is the canonical recollection-of-death (AN 6.19), here paired with the canonical anicca formula (sabbe sankhara anicca) - both are canonical objects directly assigned.

instances.object: mettā (the Karaṇīyamettasutta itself as kammaṭṭhāna), then used as pādaka for vipassanā
instances.recipient: the band of bhikkhus (te bhikkhū) sent back to the same forest grove
instances.recipient_features: monks previously driven out by tree-devas' frightening apparitions (bheravārammaṇa)
instances.occasion: the Buddha concludes the discourse and dispatches them back to the grove with the instruction to take up and develop this very kammaṭṭhāna
instances.evidence_pali: idameva kammaṭṭhānaṃ āsevatha bhāvetha bahulīkarotha, tepi vo amanussā taṃ bheravārammaṇaṃ na dassessanti
instances.warrant_basis: This is the Karaniyametta-sutta's own nidana: metta assigned to monks frightened by tree-deva apparitions in the grove - Snp 1.8 is the direct canonical warrant.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra - the skin-pentad kesā, lomā, nakhā, dantā, taco, first five of the 32 parts)
instances.recipient: the boy Tissa (the future Vanavāsītissa-sāmaṇera), a seven-year-old seeking ordination
instances.recipient_features: sattavassika (seven years old); sukhedhita (delicately raised); resolved to keep all the elder's terms
instances.occasion: the elder, before ordaining him, declares and gives him the tacapañcaka as repulsiveness-contemplation; the commentary notes this subject is never omitted by any Buddha at ordination
instances.evidence_pali: Thero ‘‘sādhū’’ti vatvā tassa paṭikūlamanasikāravasena tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā taṃ pabbājesi
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapancaka (kesa loma nakha danta taco) is the opening of the 32-parts/patikulamanasikara - a canonical body-contemplation (MN 10 / DN 22 patikula-pabba; kayagatasati MN 119).

instances.object: unspecified kammaṭṭhāna (taken from the Teacher, up to arahatta)
instances.recipient: Vanavāsītissa-sāmaṇera (a young novice)
instances.recipient_features: seven-year-old novice troubled by visiting relatives' chatter; wishes to take a meditation subject and withdraw to the forest
instances.occasion: the novice approaches the Teacher wishing to learn a meditation subject and enter the forest, to avoid relatives interrupting
instances.evidence_pali: So satthāraṃ upasaṅkamitvā vanditvā yāva arahattā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā upajjhāyaṃ vanditvā pattacīvaramādāya vihārā nikkhamitvā
instances.warrant_basis: Unspecified kammatthana, no criterion; generic take-a-subject-from-the-teacher narrative formula - no canonical object or keying to name.

instances.object: unspecified kammaṭṭhāna taken from the Teacher for vipassanā; plus Sāriputta granting higher-path kammaṭṭhāna to sekkha monks
instances.recipient: Lakuṇḍakabhaddiya (took kammaṭṭhāna from the Teacher) and the sekkha monks who request a higher-path kammaṭṭhāna from Sāriputta
instances.recipient_features: Lakuṇḍakabhaddiya: wealthy clansman, short-statured / ill-favoured, turned monk, attained sotāpatti; the trainees are sekkhas seeking the higher paths
instances.occasion: after ordaining and gaining faith he takes a subject from the Teacher and does vipassanā; trainee monks then approach Sāriputta requesting kammaṭṭhāna for the higher path
instances.evidence_pali: satthu santike kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā vipassanāya kammaṃ karonto sotāpattiphalaṃ pāpuṇi. Tadā sekkhā bhikkhū yebhuyyena āyasmantaṃ sāriputtaṃ upasaṅkamitvā uparimaggatthāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ yācanti
instances.warrant_basis: Object unspecified; generic grant/request-for-higher-path kammatthana keyed to sekkha capacity - no named canonical meditation subject or antidote pairing.

instances.object: mettā (mettaṃ eva bhāvetha)
instances.recipient: the ministers of Sīlava-mahārāja (amacce - a stated class)
instances.recipient_features: ministers bound and buried alive up to the neck by the robber-king; a well-disciplined retinue
instances.occasion: King Sīlava and his ministers, buried alive in the cremation ground by the conquering robber-king, are exhorted by the king not to harbour anger but to develop loving-kindness toward him
instances.evidence_pali: Tadā sīlavamahārājā amacce āmantetvā ‘‘corarañño upari kopaṃ akatvā mettaṃ eva bhāvetha, tātā’’ti ovadi.
instances.warrant_basis: Metta as the canonical antidote to anger toward a person who wronged you (aghatapativinaya, AN 5.161; metta-byapada in the Meghiya formula AN 9.3) - situational anger is canonically paired with metta.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra - the five skin-parts: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco)
instances.recipient: Paṇḍita-sāmaṇera (the young ordinand)
instances.recipient_features: a boy candidate for going-forth, told of the difficulty of pabbajjā, willing to follow the elder's instruction
instances.occasion: at ordination the elder describes the difficulty of going forth, then wets the hair and gives the tacapañcaka subject before ordaining him
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘tena hi ehī’’ti kese temetvā tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhitvā pabbājesi
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapancaka patikulamanasikara is the canonical first five of the 32 parts (MN 10 / DN 22 patikula-pabba; kayagatasati MN 119) - canonical object directly assigned.

instances.object: asubha + kāyagatāsati (developing nibbidā/disenchantment)
instances.recipient: Khemā (Khemātherī - the addressee in the verse)
instances.recipient_features: verse-recipient directed to develop the foul and body-directed mindfulness, abounding in disenchantment
instances.occasion: verse of instruction (Khemātherīgāthā context; the same admonition verse also given to Rāhula): develop the mind in the foul, one-pointed and well-concentrated, let body-mindfulness be present, abound in disenchantment
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehi, ekaggaṃ susamāhitaṃ; Sati kāyagatā tyatthu, nibbidā bahulā bhava.
instances.warrant_basis: Asubha and kayagatasati are canonical objects (MN 119; asubha-raga AN 9.3); the assigning verse 'Asubhaya cittam bhavehi... sati kayagata' is itself canonical (the Rahula/Thi admonition set).

instances.object: vedanāpariggaha-kammaṭṭhāna (apprehending feeling / vedanānupassanā)
instances.recipient: Sāmāvatī and her women (the stream-enterer women of the harem)
instances.recipient_features: lay female disciples, some already sotāpannas, perishing in the burning palace; taught the Dhamma by Khujjuttarā the noble disciple
instances.occasion: as the palace burns, the women, established in the fruit, attend to the feeling-apprehending meditation subject and attain higher fruits at death
instances.evidence_pali: gehe jhāyante vedanāpariggahakammaṭṭhānaṃ manasi karontiyo kāci dutiyaphalaṃ, kāci tatiyaphalaṃ pāpuṇitvā
instances.warrant_basis: Vedanapariggaha is vedananupassana, a canonical satipatthana lane (MN 10 / DN 22) - a canonical meditation subject directly assigned.

instances.object: unspecified kammaṭṭhāna given (by Sāriputta), pupil gains no distinction
instances.recipient: Sāriputta's pupil - the son of the elder's former lay friend / a brahmin's son who went forth under Sāriputta
instances.recipient_features: a wealthy brahmin's son who renounced great fortune; gains no distinction from Sāriputta's repeated instruction, recognised as buddhaveneyya
instances.occasion: Sāriputta advises and gives a meditation subject repeatedly but the pupil makes no progress; recognising he is to be guided by the Buddha, Sāriputta takes him to the Buddha
instances.evidence_pali: Tassa thero bahuso ovaditvā kammaṭṭhānamadāsi, so tena visesaṃ nādhigacchati. Tato thero ‘‘buddhaveneyyo eso’’ti ñatvā taṃ ādāya bhagavato santikaṃ gantvā
instances.warrant_basis: Object unspecified; the operative content is the buddhaveneyya capacity-diagnosis machinery (only the Buddha reads his faculties) - the teacher-diagnosis apparatus, with no named canonical object.

instances.object: unspecified kammaṭṭhāna (taken and striven at, leading to arahatta)
instances.recipient: Dhammika the lay devotee (turned monk)
instances.recipient_features: a righteous householder who goes forth after his son and wife have gone forth; takes a meditation subject and strives
instances.occasion: after going forth he takes a kammaṭṭhāna and strives, completing the renunciant task and attaining the goal
instances.evidence_pali: So kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā ghaṭento vāyamanto attano pabbajitakiccaṃ niṭṭhapetvā
instances.warrant_basis: Passive gahetva, no named giver, object, or criterion - generic take-a-subject-and-strive formula; nothing canonical to warrant a specific assignment.

instances.object: differing kammaṭṭhāna assigned to four monks - six sense-bases (saḷāyatana) / five aggregates (pañcakkhandha) / four great elements (cattāro mahābhūtā) / eighteen elements (aṭṭhārasa dhātuyo)
instances.recipient: four monks who approached the Tathāgata and requested a meditation subject
instances.recipient_features: four monks, each given a different object suited to him; each attained arahatta via his own object, prompting the question why one nibbāna is reached by differing subjects
instances.occasion: the four monks request a kammaṭṭhāna; the Teacher gives each one; they go to their day- and night-quarters and each attains arahatta by a differing object (Kiṃsukopama frame)
instances.evidence_pali: Cattāro hi bhikkhū tathāgataṃ upasaṅkamitvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ yāciṃsu, satthā tesaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi. Te kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā attano rattiṭṭhānadivāṭṭhānāni agamiṃsu.
instances.warrant_basis: The objects (salayatana/khandha/dhatu) are canonical, but the operative move is the differential per-person capacity-matching apparatus; SN 35.245 (Kimsukopama) presents distinct doors converging on nibbana yet does NOT key them to individual capacities - that keying is the commentarial addition.

instances.object: kāyagatāsati cluster - catudhātuvavatthāna, catubbidha-sampajañña, ānāpānassati, āhāre-paṭikūlasaññā (with nibbidā direction); plus pātimokkha- and indriya-saṃvara-sīla
instances.recipient: the venerable Rāhula (named)
instances.recipient_features: established in livelihood-purity and the fourfold purity-of-virtue (catupārisuddhisīla); now to be settled in the remaining virtue and in calm-and-insight
instances.occasion: the Buddha, having established Rāhula in livelihood-purity virtue, now trains him in the remaining virtue and in samatha-vipassanā with 'saṃvuto pātimokkhasmi', directing kāyagatāsati toward disenchantment
instances.evidence_pali: evaṃ catupārisuddhisīle patiṭṭhitassa tuyhaṃ catudhātuvavatthānacatubbidhasampajaññānāpānassatiāhārepaṭikūlasaññābhāvanādibhedā kāyagatā sati atthu bhavatu, bhāvehi nanti attho
instances.warrant_basis: The kayagatasati cluster (catudhatuvavatthana, anapanassati, ahare-patikulasanna) is canonical (MN 119); MN 62 (Maharahulovada) is the direct canonical warrant for teaching Rahula the elements + anapanasati.

instances.object: asubha-bhāvanā (avoiding the rāga-grounding subhanimitta; on living and dead body), one-pointed and well-concentrated
instances.recipient: the venerable Rāhula (named; second-person bhāvehi)
instances.recipient_features: directed to abandon the beautiful sign connected with lust (rāgaṭṭhāniya subhanimitta) and develop the unattractive
instances.occasion: continuing the Rāhula admonition, having shown the access plane the Buddha now shows the absorption plane: avoid the lust-grounding sign and develop the foul, one-pointed and well-concentrated
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhāya cittaṃ bhāvehīti yathā saviññāṇake aviññāṇake vā kāye asubhabhāvanā sampajjati, evaṃ cittaṃ bhāvehi.
instances.warrant_basis: Named Rāhula directed to abandon rāgaṭṭhāniya subhanimitta and develop asubha; asubha→rāga is the canonical antidote (asubhā bhāvetabbā rāgassa pahānāya).

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra - the five skin-parts)
instances.recipient: Vaṅgīsa (ordained by Nigrodhakappa at the Buddha's command)
instances.recipient_features: a brahmin seeking the skull-tapping 'spell'/vijjā, ordained because it does not succeed for the unordained; later foremost in inspired speech (paṭibhāna), attained paṭisambhidā and arahatta
instances.occasion: the Buddha orders Nigrodhakappa to ordain Vaṅgīsa; on ordaining him the elder declares the tacapañcaka subject
instances.evidence_pali: So taṃ pabbājetvā tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka is a subset of the canonical 32-parts / paṭikūlamanasikāra body-contemplation; a real canonical meditation subject.

instances.object: unspecified kammaṭṭhāna (taken from the Teacher; mind scatters, Teacher about to re-instruct)
instances.recipient: the venerable Sīha (of the Malla royal family)
instances.recipient_features: born in a Malla royal family; after going forth takes a meditation subject and dwells in the forest, but his mind runs to varied objects, is not one-pointed, and he cannot accomplish his goal
instances.occasion: having taken a kammaṭṭhāna and gone to the forest his mind scatters; the Teacher, seeing this, stands in the air to instruct him
instances.evidence_pali: So dhammaṃ sutvā paṭiladdhasaddho pabbajitvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā araññe viharati. Tassa cittaṃ nānārammaṇe vidhāvati, ekaggaṃ na hoti
instances.warrant_basis: Object is unspecified (generic kammaṭṭhāna from the Teacher); no named canonical object can be warranted.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra - the five skin-parts)
instances.recipient: the seven-year-old son of the lay supporter (ordained as Kosambivāsītissatthera's novice)
instances.recipient_features: a seven-year-old boy offered by his father to be the elder's attendant-novice; attained arahatta with the four paṭisambhidās at the shaving of the head
instances.occasion: the layman brings his son to the elder for going-forth; the elder wets the hair and gives the tacapañcaka subject at ordination
instances.evidence_pali: Athassa thero kese temetvā tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ datvā pabbājesi. So khuraggeyeva saha paṭisambhidāhi arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka = canonical body-parts / paṭikūlamanasikāra subject; the assigned object is a canonical meditation subject.

instances.object: asubha (for rāga), mettā (for byāpāda), ānāpānassati (for vitakka), plus vipassanā-nimitta (anicca/dukkha/anatta)
instances.recipient: a practitioner/yogāvacara being instructed (generic class addressed in the Tuvaṭaka-niddesa gloss)
instances.recipient_features: one afflicted by rāga / byāpāda / vitakka respectively
instances.occasion: Niddesa exegesis of the Tuvaṭakasutta directive on what the teacher declares to the practitioner (asubhakathaṃ katheti, mettābhāvanaṃ ācikkhati, ānāpānassatiṃ ācikkhati)
instances.evidence_pali: Asubhakathaṃ kathetīti uddhumātakādidasavidhaṃ asubhapaṭisaṃyuttakathaṃ ācikkhati. Vuttañhetaṃ – ‘‘asubhā bhāvetabbā rāgassa pahānāyā’’ti (a. ni. 9.1). Mettābhāvanaṃ ācikkhatīti mettābhāvanaṃ cittasinehanaṃ katheti.
instances.warrant_basis: Row quotes AN 9.1 verbatim (asubhā…rāgassa pahānāya) plus mettā→byāpāda, ānāpānassati→vitakka; the canonical antidote formula.

instances.object: vipassanā- and gantha-dhura (the Buddha redirects Upāli to dwell near him and fulfill both the insight-duty and book-duty rather than go to the forest); kammaṭṭhāna taken from the Buddha
instances.recipient: the elder Upāli (Upālitissa, the barber's son)
instances.recipient_features: newly ordained with the six Sakyans; suited to both vipassanā-dhura and gantha-dhura; future foremost in Vinaya
instances.occasion: Upāli takes a kammaṭṭhāna from the Buddha and requests forest residence; the Buddha redirects him to dwell near himself so both burdens mature
instances.evidence_pali: So pabbajitvā upasampanno hutvā satthu santike kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā ‘‘mayhaṃ, bhante, araññavāsaṃ anujānāthā’’ti āha. ‘‘Bhikkhu araññe vasantassa ekameva dhuraṃ vaḍḍhissati, mayhaṃ pana santike vasantassa vipassanādhurañca ganthadhurañca paripūressatī’’ti.
instances.warrant_basis: The vipassanā-dhura vs gantha-dhura distinction is commentarial; no canonical assignment locus, and the redirect adds no canonical object.

instances.object: brahmavihāra (and the remaining kasiṇas) - Kuddālapaṇḍita teaches the assembly the kammaṭṭhāna
instances.recipient: the ascetic assembly/retinue of Kuddālapaṇḍita (the seven kings and their followers who renounced)
instances.recipient_features: renunciants who left their kingdoms to ordain under him
instances.occasion: after establishing the hermitage, the Bodhisatta as Kuddālapaṇḍita, having mastered the kasiṇas and brahmavihāras, expounds the kammaṭṭhāna to his community (Jātaka frame)
instances.evidence_pali: Kuddālapaṇḍito sesakasiṇesupi parikammaṃ katvā brahmavihāre bhāvetvā parisāya kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi. Sabbe samāpattilābhino hutvā brahmavihāre bhāvetvā brahmalokaparāyaṇā ahesuṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Jātaka frame; generic brahmavihāra+kasiṇa teaching to an assembly with no defilement/situation keying and commentarial kasiṇa parikamma machinery.

instances.object: an unnamed kammaṭṭhāna selected as suitable (sappāya) from the 38 subjects - the Buddha investigates and teaches the fitting one
instances.recipient: a group of monks who requested a meditation subject for release from saṃsāra
instances.recipient_features: weary of existence, afraid of birth-aging-sickness-death (bhavesu ukkaṇṭhitā jātijarābyādhimaraṇabhayabhītā)
instances.occasion: the monks approach the Buddha and request a kammaṭṭhāna for liberation from saṃsāra; he selects the sappāya one from the 38
instances.evidence_pali: Satthā tesaṃ aṭṭhatiṃsāya kammaṭṭhānesu sappāyaṃ vicinitvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Unnamed sappāya subject selected from the 38-kammaṭṭhāna framework - the Vism carita/sappāya-selection machinery; no single canonical locus.

instances.object: an unnamed kammaṭṭhāna given by the Buddha to Eraka (later followed by the corrective 'dukkhā kāmā erakā' verse-ovāda)
instances.recipient: Eraka (the future elder Erakatthera)
instances.recipient_features: newly faithful, ordained out of saṃvega; later overcome by discontent (ukkaṇṭhā) and lustful/wrong thought (kāma-/micchā-vitakka)
instances.occasion: after ordination the Buddha gives him a kammaṭṭhāna; growing despondent, he is later admonished by the Buddha with a verse
instances.evidence_pali: tassa satthā kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi. So kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā katipayadivasātikkamena ukkaṇṭhābhibhūto vihāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Object unstated; commentarial biographical frame of a generic Buddha-given kammaṭṭhāna.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (the skin-pentad: kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco; paṭikūlamanasikāra)
instances.recipient: Saṃkicca, a seven-year-old novice (sāmaṇera) of Sāriputta
instances.recipient_features: seven years old at ordination; a final-life being (pacchimabhavika)
instances.occasion: relatives bring the boy to Sāriputta to ordain; the elder gives the tacapañcaka subject at the moment of head-shaving and he attains arahantship at the razor's edge
instances.evidence_pali: Thero tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ datvā pabbājesi. So khuraggeyeva saha paṭisambhidāhi arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: Tacapañcaka (kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco) = canonical body-parts / paṭikūlamanasikāra subject.

instances.object: an unnamed kammaṭṭhāna taken from the Buddha by Mahāpāla; he later pursues (sukkha-)vipassanā
instances.recipient: Mahāpāla (the future elder Cakkhupāla), son of the householder Mahāsuvaṇṇa
instances.recipient_features: householder's elder son; ordained from faith after hearing the Dhamma; later took up dry-insight despite eye-disease
instances.occasion: after five years and the rains-residence/pavāraṇā, Mahāpāla takes a kammaṭṭhāna from the Buddha and goes with sixty companions to a frontier forest dwelling to practice
instances.evidence_pali: vuṭṭhavasso pavāretvā satthu santike kammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā saṭṭhimatte sahāyabhikkhū labhitvā tehi saddhiṃ bhāvanānukūlaṃ vasanaṭṭhānaṃ pariyesanto
instances.warrant_basis: Object unstated (generic kammaṭṭhāna received from the Buddha); biographical frame, no nameable canonical object.

instances.object: maraṇasati / maraṇassati (mindfulness of death) - the Bodhisatta directs the terrified king to develop it
instances.recipient: the king (rājā), addressed by the Bodhisatta-teacher
instances.recipient_features: struck by fear of death (maraṇabhayabhīta) after hearing of the swift perishing of life-formations; fainted and regained mindfulness
instances.occasion: after the teaching on the swift dissolution of rūpa/nāma the king faints from death-fear; the Bodhisatta revives and exhorts him to develop maraṇasati and do meritorious deeds
instances.evidence_pali: Atha naṃ mahāsatto ‘‘mahārāja, mā bhāyi, maraṇassatiṃ bhāvehi, dhammaṃ carāhi, dānādīni puññāni karohi, appamatto hohi, devā’’ti ovadi.
instances.warrant_basis: Maraṇasati directed to one struck by death-fear; mindfulness of death is a canonical recollection and the situational pairing is its canonical use.

instances.object: asubha / tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (five-skin foulness contemplation)
instances.recipient: Kappa (a man moved to bodily disgust who then asked to ordain, given the subject at ordination)
instances.recipient_features: aṭṭīyamāno harāyamāno jigucchamāno saṃviggahadayo - disgusted with and agitated about his own body after hearing the Buddha's asubhakathā
instances.occasion: after the Buddha gave him a discourse on foulness, Kappa asked to go forth; the Buddha directed a monk to ordain him, who gave him the tacapañcaka at ordination
instances.evidence_pali: So taṃ tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ datvā pabbājesi.
instances.warrant_basis: Asubha/tacapañcaka (canonical body-parts subject) given to one disgusted with the body after asubhakathā; object canonical, keying aligns with AN 9.1 asubha→rāga.

instances.object: caritānukūla-kammaṭṭhāna (a meditation subject suited to his temperament)
instances.recipient: Pilindavaccha (a brahmin spell-master who ordained intending to learn the great Gandhāra spell)
instances.recipient_features: upanissayasampanna; a former vijjādhara whose lesser spell failed in the Buddha's presence, who ordained believing the going-forth was the spell's preliminary
instances.occasion: after the Buddha taught him Dhamma upon his ordination, the Buddha gave a temperament-suited meditation subject; he soon established insight and reached arahatship
instances.evidence_pali: Tassa bhagavā dhammaṃ kathetvā caritānukūlaṃ kammaṭṭhānaṃ adāsi.
instances.warrant_basis: Explicit 'caritānukūla' temperament-matching - the carita-typing machinery itself; rests only on the commentarial carita matrix, no canonical basis.

instances.object: mettā / mettābhāvanā (loving-kindness)
instances.recipient: Sopāka (a seven-year-old boy ordained in a charnel-ground setting)
instances.recipient_features: sattavassika dārako; pubbahetunā codiyamāno, pasannamānaso; dwelt in the charnel ground (susāna)
instances.occasion: after ordaining the boy at his father's request, the Buddha set him to the cultivation of loving-kindness; he then dwelt in the charnel ground, made mettā-jhāna the base, developed insight and reached arahatship
instances.evidence_pali: taṃ pabbājetvā bhagavā mettābhāvanāya niyojesi. So mettākammaṭṭhānaṃ gahetvā susāne viharanto
instances.warrant_basis: Mettābhāvanā is a canonical meditation subject (Karaṇīyamettasutta); criterion unstated, plain assignment of a canonical object.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified) - declared by an elder
instances.recipient: Sumaṅgala (a poor farmer's son who ordained after seeing the honour shown to monks)
instances.recipient_features: daliddakule nibbatti - born to a poor family, made his living by ploughing; ordained out of admiration for the monks' comfort
instances.occasion: a great elder, out of compassion, ordained him and declared a meditation subject; he later cultivated insight and reached arahatship
instances.evidence_pali: So taṃ karuṇāyanto pabbājetvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi.
instances.warrant_basis: Object unnamed; bare elder-to-pupil kammaṭṭhāna handover (apadāna formula), nothing to warrant.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified) - declared to his pupils
instances.recipient: the 44,000 resident pupils (antevāsika) of the ascetic Nanda (a past life of Subhūti)
instances.recipient_features: antevāsikā - resident pupil-ascetics; their teacher had himself gained the eight attainments and five higher knowledges
instances.occasion: as an ascetic teacher (pre-Buddha era) he declared a meditation subject to his pupils, who soon became jhāna-attainers
instances.evidence_pali: Antevāsikānampi kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi. Tepi nacirasseva jhānalābhino ahesuṃ.
instances.warrant_basis: Pre-Buddha ascetic teacher declares an UNNAMED kammaṭṭhāna to pupils; no named canonical object and no defilement keying - standard commentarial teacher-to-pupil formula.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified), developed into mettā-jhāna as base for vipassanā
instances.recipient: Subhūti (the householder Subhūti, who ordained after hearing the Dhamma with Anāthapiṇḍika)
instances.recipient_features: subhūtikuṭumbiko - a householder who gained faith on hearing the Dhamma; had mastered the two mātikās after ordination
instances.occasion: after ordaining and mastering the two mātikās he had a meditation subject expounded, then produced mettā-jhāna, made it the base, developed insight and reached arahatship
instances.evidence_pali: So upasampanno dve mātikā paguṇā katvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ kathāpetvā araññe samaṇadhammaṃ karonto mettājhānaṃ nibbattetvā taṃ pādakaṃ katvā vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā arahattaṃ pāpuṇi.
instances.warrant_basis: The ASSIGNED kammaṭṭhāna is unnamed; mettā-jhāna emerges in practice as a pādaka, not as a keyed assignment to a criterion. Apadāna-style formula, no canonical object↔situation pairing.

instances.object: kammaṭṭhāna (unspecified) - declared by the Buddha, leading through the eight attainments and five abhiññās to vipassanā
instances.recipient: Sunīta (a low-born refuse/night-soil scavenger whom the Buddha personally encouraged and ordained as ehibhikkhu)
instances.recipient_features: jātiyā kammassa ca nihīnatāya - of low birth and lowly occupation, ashamed and timid; the Buddha deliberately roused his self-confidence (vesārajja)
instances.occasion: the Buddha led him to the monastery and declared a meditation subject; he produced the eight attainments and five abhiññās, developed insight, and became a sixfold-knowledge arahat
instances.evidence_pali: Bhagavā taṃ vihāraṃ netvā kammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi. So paṭhamaṃ aṭṭha samāpattiyo, pañca ca abhiññāyo nibbattetvā vipassanaṃ vaḍḍhetvā chaḷabhiñño ahosi.
instances.warrant_basis: Buddha gives Sunīta an UNSPECIFIED kammaṭṭhāna keyed to low-birth 'situation'; no named canonical object and the canon supplies no object↔caste pairing.

instances.object: asubha / aṭṭhisaṅghāta (charnel-ground / fresh-corpse foulness contemplation)
instances.recipient: five hundred over-estimating (adhimānika) monks who mistook jhāna for arahatship
instances.recipient_features: adhimānika - had attained jhāna and presumed the path-task done; latent rāga undetected until they saw fresh corpses and recognized their unpurified state
instances.occasion: the Buddha refused to receive them and sent them to the raw charnel ground (āmakasusāna); confronting fresh corpses they aroused rāga, recognized their defilement, whereupon the Buddha radiated light and spoke a verse
instances.evidence_pali: āmakasusānaṃ gantvā tato āgantvā maṃ passantū
instances.warrant_basis: asubha / charnel-ground (aṭṭhisaṅghāta) as the antidote to latent rāga in the adhimānika monks - the Meghiya antidote (asubha→rāga, AN 9.3) plus navasivathika DN 22 / MN 10. Named canonical object + canonical defilement keying.

instances.object: cattāro brahmavihārā (mettā, karuṇā) + kasiṇaparikamma
instances.recipient: the person the Bodhisatta consoles (his interlocutor/son in the jātaka)
instances.recipient_features: distressed, needing reassurance (assāsetvā)
instances.occasion: the Bodhisatta consoles him, then teaches the four brahmavihāras and kasiṇa-preparation
instances.evidence_pali: ‘‘hotu, tāta, ehi tvaṃ mettaṃ bhāvehi, karuṇaṃ bhāvehī’’ti cattāro brahmavihāre ācikkhi, ācikkhitvā kasiṇaparikammaṃ ācikkhi
instances.warrant_basis: mettā for a distressed/fearful person is the canonical Karaṇīyamettasutta keying

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra of the skin-pentad)
instances.recipient: Candābha, a newly ordained former brahmin
instances.recipient_features: former luminous-glow brahmin who lost his glow before the Buddha; deeply faithful (pasannacitto)
instances.occasion: an elder ordains him at the Buddha's instruction, then teaches the meditation subject
instances.evidence_pali: So taṃ pabbājetvā tacapañcakakammaṭṭhānaṃ ācikkhi. So vipassanaṃ ārabhitvā na cireneva arahattaṃ patvā
instances.warrant_basis: tacapañcaka paṭikūlamanasikāra (skin-pentad of the body parts) at ordination - a canonical meditation subject: Khp 3 body-parts / DN 22 / MN 10 paṭikūlamanasikāra / MN 119 kāyagatāsati. Standard ordination subject, not carita-typed.

instances.object: maraṇasati (maraṇassati)
instances.recipient: the people of Āḷavī (a lay class), notably a 16-year-old weaver's daughter who takes it up
instances.recipient_features: lay congregation at an almsgiving; one young weaver's daughter seizes the instruction and practices it for years
instances.occasion: the Buddha's anumodanā after a meal at Āḷavī, exhorting them to develop mindfulness of death
instances.evidence_pali: avassaṃ mayā maritabbameva, maraṇapariyosānaṃ me jīvitaṃ, jīvitameva aniyataṃ, maraṇaṃ niyatanti evaṃ maraṇassatiṃ bhāvetha
instances.warrant_basis: maraṇassati exhorted to the Āḷavī laity - the canonical mindfulness-of-death subject (AN 6.19 / AN 8.73); the develop-mindfulness-of-death formula matches the canonical teaching.

instances.object: tacapañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna (paṭikūlamanasikāra of the five)
instances.recipient: the young Saṃkicca, a 7-year-old boy brought to the Dhammasenāpati (Sāriputta) for ordination
